Would not advise booking with this airline. Basically called a liar by two of the cabin crew. As an anxious person, it was crippling enough to speak up in the first place, but to be made out to be lying over a £5 meal is insane. I booked return flights for my family from London Gatwick to Budapest, but after the booking was confirmed, we discovered that the flights had been changed to Luton Airport instead. What’s worse, the airline’s app still showed “LGW” for both outbound and inbound flights, which is completely misleading. Only when you click into the inbound details does it reveal Luton — a major and unacceptable discrepancy.I called customer service and waited nearly 40 minutes just to speak to someone. When I finally got through to a representative named Mr. Dmytro, he was extremely rude, dismissive, and unhelpful. I calmly explained the issue, but he showed no willingness to assist. He kept insisting it was my fault and told me to contact the booking company. His tone was more like a threat than customer service — saying things like “if you have no other questions, I will hang up” and then actually did just that within 10 minutes.Absolutely no attempt to resolve the problem or even act like he cared. I’ve never been treated so poorly by any airline representative. This kind of customer service is a joke — and a warning sign.This was my first and definitely last time dealing with this airline. I don’t care how good the in-flight service may be — if the ground support is this bad, it’s not worth the risk.Avoid at all costs.

Appalling service from Wizz. During the Gatwick South terminal shutdown on Friday 22nd of November, the plane took off leaving 30+ passengers at the security gate. The gate was announced only 10 minutes before and the airport had just recently reopened not giving enough time to go through security. The person at the gate was despicable and got angry to the group trying to find a solution to get one the plane as it was still there, take off in 20 minutes.Very disappointing service, I will never use Wizz again, avoid if you can.
No customer service, basically scammed us into paying more, just because they told us to check in by a date and then couldn’t even let us check in cause their website shows an error code.

The wort company I had ever fly - airport in Lisbon is disaster - one stand for give your language?!?!?!?!?! How you treat your customer!!!!!!!! Drama company keep as far as you can !!!!!!!!

The staff on Landvetter said "your problem", some of the lowprice companys do this and also asked if I can provide screenshots that it didnt work. Absolutely ridiculous service and a complete rip off, they will charge you for every item and their web / app check in doest work and ended up paying €50 per person x3  as we weren’t check in due to their webpage being down. Do not use.

Dear Mr J Viradi,I am writing to you directly to express my deep dissatisfaction with the way Wizz Air has handled my recent complaint on behalf of my family and myself. The conduct of your company throughout this process has been nothing short of disgraceful. Your complaints team has demonstrated dishonesty, a complete lack of accountability, and a shocking disregard for the seriousness of the incident we experienced.The response we received from Wizz Air claimed the matter was "resolved" and stated that we had voluntarily cancelled our flight. This is categorically false.We were denied boarding after being verbally abused and threatened by two of your gate agents. We did not cancel our flight. We were forced not to travel due to the unacceptable and intimidating behaviour of your staff. Furthermore, for such a serious incident, which involved a police report, CCTV footage, witnesses, and medical visits following the event, we received no meaningful explanation, no investigation, and no opportunity to appeal. The claim was simply closed, as though our distress and experience were irrelevant.Let me be absolutely clear: I have witness statements, CCTV evidence, and a police report number. I will not allow this matter to be swept under the rug. We have made repeated attempts to seek resolution through your complaints procedure, only to be met with evasive responses and a total lack of accountability.I demand to be compensated for all of the distress, harm and time wasted this has caused I would like to know what disciplinary action, if any, has been taken against the staff involved. These individuals should not be allowed to continue interacting with the public, as their actions were not only unprofessional, but dangerous. Their conduct caused extreme distress to my family, ruined our holiday, and left us shaken.This experience reflects directly on your leadership and the standards set across Wizz Air, both at the customer service level and among your ground staff at Gatwick. If this is representative of how your airline treats passengers, especially those in need of medical care or compassion, then your company is failing on the most basic level of decency.I have logged an investigation with my credit card company for the cost of the tickets as my husband paid for the flights and didn't receive the good /services. I yet have been left with other purchases such as flights and hotels food etc... a result of being denied boarding which you are responsible to compensate together with all the distress caused.If I do not receive a proper investigation and compensation for the emotional damages caused, I will take this public. I have access to a large network of respected individuals in the media, including senioriournalists, editors, and newspaper owners who would be more than willing to give this story the attention it deserves.I would appreciate your response.Please refer to your email located on line it’s your gmail .

let me have it I try to do it online and it didn't work I tried to speak to a representative and it took forever until  I could find their customer service number. when I finally did the customer service agent couldn't have been worst!!! On January 29, Wizz Air cancelled the Venice–Barcelona flight in the middle of the night, leaving families with children stranded, without proper assistance, in the cold, and with the first available alternative flight only on January 3.Faced with this unacceptable situation, I was forced to purchase another flight at my own expense.I am now trying to claim reimbursement for the expenses incurred and the compensation provided under EU Regulation EC 261/2004, but it is practically impossible to submit a complaint:the Wizz Air account automatically logs me out every time I try to access the complaint sectionthe phone number +34 800 600 261, advertised as free, does not provide real support: agents ask to call again or an automated message reports that all lines are busyThis situation gives the impression of a system designed to prevent passengers from exercising their rights.Extremely negative experience. EUROPE.....PLEASE READ REVIEWS! Nobody else anywhere in the developed world would allow this lack of service, rudeness, incompetence, obvious lack of training... not to mention the obvious safety defects.that allow a burning type smell in the cabin to be normal.

Sort yourselves or Wizz Air! I will never book a flight with you again. I was just in the process of booking flights from Leeds to Bucharest. I am writing this review with deep disappointment regarding a recent experience my husband and I had during a flight with your airline. While we understand that airline staff often work under pressure, we were genuinely shocked by how the situation was handled by certain members of the crew.My husband was spoken to in an unprofessional and aggressive manner by one of the crew members, who made inappropriate and hostile remarks, including saying, “I’m not here to help you” and “I’ll show you.” This behaviour was completely uncalled for and created a very uncomfortable and distressing situation.saying( I will show you)  means  he is threating my husband What was even more troubling is that the crew leader did not give us the opportunity to explain what had happened. Instead, she appeared to side with her colleague without hearing our side, which only escalated the tension and led to my husband being removed from the flight. This decision felt unfair and unsupported by any clear reason.As a result, my husband had to book another flight at his own expense, which added both emotional and financial strain to our journey.We believe that every passenger deserves to be treated with dignity and fairness, regardless of any misunderstandings or miscommunications. We hope the airline will take this feedback seriously and ensure that all staff members are held to the highest standards of professionalism and customer care.We are open to further discussion if needed and would appreciate a proper investigation into this matter. we need a serious and fast investigation about this   I am more than happy to mention the flight number with the crew names

Dear Wizz Air,I am extremely disappointed with the experience I had today. My flight to Lithuania was originally scheduled for 14:20 but was delayed to 15:20. I arrived at the gate at 15:00, only to find it already closed. This happened at Eindhoven Airport.I attempted to contact Wizz Air through your website, but every time a representative responded, they quickly closed the conversation before resolving my issue. As a result, I am still stranded at Eindhoven Airport.This level of service is unacceptable. I expect an explanation and a resolution to this situation. Based on this experience, I will not be flying with Wizz Air again.

They now charge for calls, so it's no longer free. There's no live chat support either. It's clear they’re becoming a greedy company. They recently launched the 'All You Can Fly' promotion with enticing terms, only to later change the terms and conditions after people had already paid. This kind of bait-and-switch tactic is misleading and unethical. It’s disappointing to see a company stoop to such practices.

Wizz Air has been the worst airline I’ve ever flown with. I received a message (in Hungarian, which I don’t speak) saying my flight was delayed by two hours. Based on that, I arrived at the airport accordingly—only to be denied check-in and told it had closed before I even arrived.Despite the delay being their fault, I was forced to pay £120 for a new flight. Shockingly, they resold my original seat to another passenger who had experienced the exact same issue. This shows a complete lack of integrity and coordination.To make matters worse, there’s no proper way to file a complaint—no working email, just a form and a chat system that disconnects. It feels like they’ve made it intentionally difficult to hold them accountable.Wizz Air’s handling of passengers is unacceptable. If I could give zero stars, I would. They should be investigated and penalised for these practices.
Utterly awful. Hidden fees awkward check in. Believe me Ryanair look awesome compared. Check in online didn't work. And then you get charged for the inconvenience.

I was denied boarding on my flight from Turkey to London because Wizz Air had overbooked the flight. I know this happens as I’ve worked at an airport before, but it’s still such a greedy and unfair practice. I didn’t hold it against them at first and just wanted my compensation sorted.I filled out the online form and was contacted by a staff member named Anatolii Engovatov, who was initially very responsive. On the 9th of October, he confirmed they had everything they needed from me. I thanked him and asked when the payment would be released, but they completely ignored my emails on the 9th, 10th and 13th. After getting no reply for days, I called customer service and was on hold for over an hour and a half. When I finally got through to a lady named Vasant, she was rude, impatient, and clearly didn’t care. When I asked her to wait two minutes while I got my booking reference, she said, “You’ve got one minute, I’ve got to go, the line is busy,” even though I had waited 90 minutes to speak to her. When I asked what department she worked in, she refused to say, only replying that she “works on the phones.”After wasting almost two hours, I still had no update. Out of frustration, I emailed again on 16th October saying I would be leaving a one-star review, and within 22 minutes I suddenly got this reply from Anatolii:“Dear Clients,The payment can be released within 30 days.Best regards,Anatolii EngovatovCustomer Service Department.”So after weeks of being ignored, they only replied when I mentioned a bad review. That says everything about how Wizz Air treats its customers. Both people I dealt with showed zero care or professionalism. I was left stranded in another country, lost a day’s pay from work, and had to chase them endlessly for what I was owed. One star is being generous.

I was using Wizz for last 5 years and it was like every lowcost company, but as from last year till now their unprofessionalism is beyond the charts! Constant delays, and we talk about 6-9 hours at times! But today was a cherry on top: while mobile boarding pass are not accepted in Zvartnotz airport, and I'm forced to go through an ordianary check in airport for last 3 years, today Wizz fined me 60 eur for not passing mobile check in claiming it's mandatory, although I have never passed mobile check in this airport for last 3 years and it was fine, but today is a No No, just a lame excuse to get extra money. I spent thousand plus on Wizz, they will not see a penny from now on!

This has been one of the worst travel experiences I’ve had. It appears that Wizz Air is selling more tickets than there are seats available on the aircraft. Passengers pay for their tickets, plan their travel, and even purchase additional services like auto check-in, yet when the time comes to travel there is confusion and uncertainty about whether you will actually be able to board the flight you paid for.This kind of practice is unacceptable. Customers should not have to deal with stress and uncertainty because an airline chooses to oversell flights. It shows a complete lack of respect for passengers’ time, money, and travel plans.I paid for my ticket and additional services expecting a smooth process, not to be put in a situation where I feel like my seat may not even exist despite having a confirmed booking.This matter will not simply be ignored. I will be pursuing this further and will be seeking compensation for the inconvenience and disruption caused. Airlines must be held accountable when they fail to provide the service that customers have paid for.Very disappointing and certainly not the level of service passengers deserve.

Just rubbish! I would highly avoid. Never flying with them again. Literally got scammed to pay €70 euros at the gate due to having not having my 10kg bag checked in. I paid for it online before my flight but all because it wasn’t checked in I got charged money. Not even angry at this point more disappointed that they are able to do this to people.
We bought tickets then for some reason we have to cancel them now its been more then 6 weeks and they are not issuing our refund customer service not helping at all.

Do not use. This airlines hidden charges are absolutely ridiculous. Me and my husband ended up spending approx £200 extra due to their hidden fees. They give you the worst seats so you pay for better. People were separated from their children because they chose random allocation. At least put people together in your randomised option. They charge you to check in with someone just writing in a note book when you could have checked in yourself without having to que up in ridiculous long lines. DO NOT use this total rip off airline. This airline targets vulnerable people who need to travel on a budget. Pay your hard earned money to other airlines.

shame i cannot leave a 0 star review to this scam company.purchased 3 tickets for me my wife and a 2 month old baby, from uk to Romania and return. checked in without buying priority and guess what ...I was designated seat 4 and she with the baby seat 27, not even a change to ask someone to change seats.For return even better I got seat 9 and she got 34 with the baby, so I had to go and purchase a seat but they would pretend that no other seats were available either close to her or close to me, only to make me pay for 2 seats and not just 1. I changed my mind and refused to pay for 2 seats only to come back few minutes later and check again when suddenly there were seats available next to me (2 seats) and close to her as well. Very cheap tricks to make you pay more.the sad thing is this is the only option for us to use so unfortunately we will have no choice but to use them again. And so it looks like even if a company has 1 star average on trustpilot they still survive because or probably unfair advantage.

I booked a ticket with Wizz Plus Fare, ensuring baggage and priority, but Wizz Air’s system issued it incorrectly under Base Fare, removing my benefits and forcing me to rebook. I had to pay £516.98 extra due to their error.Their ODR system failed when I tried to dispute, and customer service ignored my complaint. I have now escalated my case to AviationADR.Wizz Air, if you care about your customers, resolve this issue and refund my money!

The only flight that doesn't get the permission to land at Dalaman airport due to forest fire. Every other airline land without forest fire issues. We've checked. Diverted to Antalya, and the only option they provide is a four hours bus ride back to Dalaman. Pathetic airline. Avoid at all cost. Update: the whole thing was a scam. They diverted us to Antalya. Told us there are coaches outside waiting to take us to Dalaman airport. Don't use this airline. They are cheap for a reason. Then when we got to the airport counter they told us we haven't checked in and made us pay the fee for not checking in (ticket no longer cheap). They took zero responsibility and did not help to rectify. I would give them ZERO STARS if I could. Checking is a right for passengers and Wizzair should fix their online system.

Do not do business with this racist and discriminatory company. Their priority is taking as much money as possible, while treating special assistance passengers unfairly and offering no support to those seated at the back of the plane.

And at no point have I had any communication from the airline. I’ve chased multiple times. Never flying wizz air again. Worst airline and worst customer experience I’ve ever had

I have never in my life experienced such appalling treatment as I did with WizzAir on November 4, 2024, at Bucharest Henri Coandă Airport. What was supposed to be a simple flight from Bucharest to Paris turned into a nightmare of harassment, intimidation, and unethical overcharging by the WizzAir ground staff.Whilst we were in queue for the check-in, my family and I were targeted by two extremely rude ground staff members who appeared to have no visible identification badges, and when asked for their names, they mumbled and even tried to cover their badges to remain anonymous. The staff member we believe was named “Alexandra Petra” approached us aggressively, demanding we check the size of our bags. We complied immediately, but as we tried to rearrange our items to make them fit, the hostility escalated.We were threatened that our belongings would be thrown in the trash if we even attempted to adjust the contents of our bags. When my partner, who had already passed through, came back to check on us, he was aggressively told he had no right to be there or talk with us and was threatened with missing the flight himself. It was an appalling display of abuse of power, and the staff were openly hostile, intimidating, and entirely unprofessional.What added to the stress and frustration was being coerced into paying for our bags at a supposedly “total” which turned out to be a lie. We were charged more than what we were told the price was, and when questioned, the staff dismissively blamed it on “airport taxes” without any explanation. The lack of transparency was deeply unethical.To make matters worse, we were told our bags would fit in the overhead cabin, but when we boarded, the overhead compartments were full, and we were forced to store our bags under the seats. Ironically, the bags that allegedly couldn’t fit under the seat (according to the ground staff) fit perfectly, further proving we had been unnecessarily charged.This experience was beyond unprofessional—it felt discriminatory, as we were treated like nuisances and spoken to as if we were less than human. The sense of xenophobia and disdain was palpable, and I left feeling humiliated and deeply disturbed.I am a WizzAir Club member, but after this horrifying experience, I will seriously reconsider ever flying with WizzAir again. I demand accountability, an apology, and a full refund for the unjust fees we were forced to pay.WizzAir, you have lost my trust, and I am appalled by the level of misconduct from your staff. To all potential travelers: BEWARE of the abusive treatment at the hands of this airline’s staff. No passenger deserves to be harassed, threatened, and ripped off.

Upon arrival from Venice at Athens airport, my checked baggage was not delivered, and I immediately filed a loss report at the lost baggage office (PIR report). After waiting more than three weeks, the baggage was finally declared permanently lost.I contacted Wizz Air repeatedly and submitted all required documentation, including a detailed list of the contents. Wizz Air offered a disproportionately low compensation compared to the actual value of the items, which was calculated based on cargo limits rather than passenger baggage...According to the Montreal Convention (1999), compensation for lost passenger baggage should be calculated based on the value of its contents, not cargo limits.Despite my efforts to resolve the matter amicably and request a reassessment of compensation, there has been no meaningful response from the airline.I would not recommend anyone to travel with that company. They are completely unreliable.

I booked a return flight from Luton airport to Sphinx airport in Cairo, Egypt. Flight No. W9 5318 on 7th Dec 2024. Flight confirmation code: GW78JX. The return flight, from Sphinx to Luton, which was scheduled for 7th Dec., was cancelled and we were stranded in Cairo. Apparently Wizzair doesn’t have a company representative in Sphinx airport, but there is a third party company which does the work for Wizzair.When the return flight was cancelled, the staff in Sphinx airport were VERY really very UNhelpful. They told us lies. They said that they will ‘rebook’ flights for us and we don’t need to do anything. But that was lie. And they said that we will be taken to a hotel and we can stay in the hotel until we can travel back to the UK. That was lie too actually they booked only one night for us in the hotel even though the earliest flight back from Cairo to Luton was 3 day away. We were told that we will have full board in the hotel. That was lie too. Actually Wizzair representatives booked only breakfast for us.So, we (the passengers of the cancelled flight) had to have very long and stressful discussion with the hotel staff to convince them that we need to stay at least 3 days in the hotel and not only one night, then we had to have another extremely stressful talks with the hotel staff just to get meals. All this should have been arranged by Wizzair without our (passengers) involvement. But unfortunately Wizzair representatives didn’t show up at the hotel at all, and we didn’t have any way to communicate with them there in Cairo. We were absolutely stranded in a hotel in a foreign country. We were only able to talk to the hotel staff and the hotel staff used to communicate with the airline via a third party company. We spent about 2 full days talking and demanding our rights to the staff in the hotel who they passed the details to the third party company who in turn passed the details to Wizzair. It was very stressful situation, especially when the hotel staff kept telling us that we need to vacate our rooms and check out, when Wizzair representatives didn’t even respond to the hotel staff, initially. It was the worst experience I have ever had with an airline. And I do travel a lot to different countries with different airlines.Eventually, and after 2 days of very difficult and stressful talks with the hotel staff, Wizzair accepted to extend our stay by 3 days and we were offered breakfast and dinner only (no lunch). Well, that should have been arranged at the beginning when we first arrived at the hotel.In the meantime very big thank you to the hotel staff, who had to bear and handle all this trouble. P.S. on the flight there were families with very young babies who suffered the most as we didn’t know when the hotel staff are going to throw us out just because Wizzair was not responding to the hotel communications.Regarding booking a replacement return flight, that is a whole story by itself. So, I had to pay £92 to buy only a return Wizzair flight back to Luton. Now 1 month has passed and I still haven’t got a refund back from Wizzair, even though I sent Wizzair 2 emails and I filled a claim form. It seems I am going to have to contact the UK AviationADR. Well, but, I have to wait for 8 weeks before I can contact AviationADR. Oh, that is not to mention I lost my National Express Coaches ticket which I was supposed to take at Luton airport on 7th Dec. to take me back home …I will never use Wizzair again, not because of the flight cancellation but because of the way we were treated by Wizzair staff while we were stranded in a foreign country.

⚠️ WARNING – MISLEADING BAGGAGE CHARGES ⚠️I prepaid a 10kg cabin bag with Wizz Air. The bag weighed 9.5kg, fully within the allowed limit. At the airport, I was charged an additional €75. The staff explicitly told me the reason was that the bag had “not been checked in”.After contacting Wizz Air customer service, the company claimed that cabin baggage does not require check-in and that the charge must therefore relate to non-compliance — despite the fact that the bag was never weighed or measured in my presence, and no rule violation was demonstrated.Wizz Air later admitted that the verbal explanation at the airport “may have caused confusion”, yet still refused both a refund and a voucher, relying on vague internal “operational assessments” that passengers are not allowed to verify.This is a classic example of misleading and non-transparent charging practices. Even when you pay in advance and comply with the rules, you may still be charged arbitrarily with no proof and no accountability.Passengers should be extremely cautious when flying with Wizz Air.

I was satisfied with how things went with the last 2 or 3 times I flew with Wizz Air. But there are a few things about how the company does things which I find not good, and is a reason why I will avoid Wizz Air in the future. I tend to book flights very early to get a good price, and it has happened twice now in the last few months where Wizz Air cancelled the flight on the day I was to travel, rebooking me on a flight either a day earlier or a day later. Firstly, this is very annoying because this means to travel on my originally booked day, I need to find another flight but now I am doing the booking much closer to the date of my flight, and therefore the new flight becomes more expensive than it could have been had I booked the other airline in the first place instead of Wizz Air. This lack of stability in their projected flight schedules, if it happens often, is a no go with me. The second problem is the flaw that they have in their refund policy when they have cancelled my fight. At first they give a flight credit which must be used within 1 year. Now, this can be refunded 100% upon request, but here comes the flaw in their policy: It can be refunded only if the cancelled flight had not been partly or fully paid with a flight credit from a previously cancelled flight. So here is the scenario I have stumbled into: They cancelled my first flight, I accepted the flight credit, and used it to pay for a second flight (different date and destination). And now they cancelled the second flight. And so according to their rules, I cannot get a refund for the second cancelled flight, and must find a way to use the flight credit within one year. Which is not easy as I generally do not travel where Wizz Air flies.

However, when booking with Wizz Air, I had not only paid for flight cancellation or delay protection, but also a 'self transfer guarantee', meaning that if one leg of the flight was delayed causing me to miss the next leg, then I had the option of either a refund for the first flight, a flight for the rest of there trip organised by the airline, or a flight back to my original destination. As soon as I knew I had to rebook, I tried to call the airline and AirHelp (the site  for customer service I was directed to contact by their website) but had no-one pick up for 45 minutes. I then sent an email to AirHelp, but did not receive a reply for 18 hours. During this time, I was stranded alone in Jeddah airport with no other way of contacting the airline, and then had to fork out $2100 for another 2 flights to get me back to Perth, Australia. I instantly lodged a claim to have my flight reimbursed, thinking that as outlined and promised in the policy, I would get my money back. However Wizz refund me for the first leg of the flight, and refused to pay any more. I was left $1500 out of pocket for their mistake, plus the cost of airport food over the course of 24 hours that I had been stranded there - another cost that was promised to be reimbursed in the case of a flight delay. I have now been emailing and calling the company for an entire year, receiving slow, unhelpful replies, after submitting every peace of supporting documentation required to make my case, reciting their own policies back to them and being told I was correct, but they still could not process my request. I've still been neglected in terms of response, am now taking legal action.Never EVER book with this airline, you will get completely ripped off if absolutely anything goes wrong.

I will just quote my complaint to the company, it gives full review of lack of responsibility and indifference to customersThis letter serves as a formal notice of a severe service failure and a demand for immediate financial rectification regarding the unilateral cancellation of our flight from Kutaisi to Abu Dhabi, scheduled for August 30, 2025, under booking reference PNRLGQ.Last night, you summarily cancelled this flight and, without our consent, converted the ticket value into Wizz Air credit—a currency that is utterly useless to us.Let's be unequivocally clear: we are residents of the UAE. We purchased these tickets for our family vacation at a time when your organization was fully aware of its impending withdrawal from the UAE market. Your decision to continue selling tickets for a service you knew was slated for termination is, at best, a disingenuous business practice and, at worst, deliberately deceptive. This is further evidenced by the prior schedule change to our outbound Abu Dhabi-Kutaisi flight, a modification made when your market exit strategy was already in motion.To compound this, your ground staff's silence on the matter was deafening. During our check-in process at Abu Dhabi airport on August 16, 2025, for the outbound flight to Kutaisi, your representatives had full access to our entire itinerary, including our UAE residency details and the return flight information. Despite the cancellation decision being, at that point, an operational certainty within your system, we were met with a complete and total failure to inform. This omission was not an oversight; it was a conscious decision to withhold critical information from a family about to be stranded.Your actions have had severe and cascading consequences. Our family, which includes a young child, has not only had our vacation plans systematically dismantled but has also been subjected to significant financial and moral damages. We have been forced to procure alternative, substantially more expensive tickets from a different departure city. This has triggered a cascade of additional, unbudgeted costs, including but not limited to: new ground transportation, supplementary hotel bookings, and non-refundable losses on existing reservations due to the forced alteration of our itinerary.Furthermore, the operational procedure of your notification is unacceptable. An email announcing the cancellation was dispatched at 2:00 AM—a time when you can reasonably expect recipients to be asleep. By 2:53 AM, you had already taken the liberty of unilaterally seizing our funds and converting them into a proprietary credit we cannot use. This action is not just procedurally deficient; it is ethically reprehensible.This brings me to your fundamentally flawed customer communication protocol. Despite possessing our direct contact numbers, you opt for email notifications—a channel you yourselves systematically compromise by flooding it with a daily deluge of marketing spam. You have single-handedly created an environment where critical operational messages are indistinguishable from unsolicited advertising, ensuring they are ignored or unread. To presume that any customer on vacation will dedicate hours to sifting through your marketing refuse in search of a critical update is absurd.To summarize the damages directly resulting from your decisions:Direct Financial Losses: We have incurred substantial costs for new flights, new accommodations, penalty fees from previous bookings, and logistical transport to a new airport.Severe Disruption and Inconvenience: You have forced a complete restructuring of our vacation itinerary and travel plans.Moral and Psychological Distress: Your actions have caused significant stress and hardship for my family, including my child.

I have flown with many airlines, but Wizz Air is by far the worst. Here’s why:1. Ridiculous Pricing: For what they offer, the prices are unacceptable. Awful Cabin Comfort: The legroom feels below the EU minimum standards. You are crammed in like sardines. It’s genuinely uncomfortable.4. Poor Communication & Organization: Today, shortly after boarding, they announced a one-hour delay. If they had informed us earlier, we could have waited at the gate in reasonable conditions, but instead we had to sit inside the plane.5. Excessive Fees: I was charged an outrageous €60 at the airport for my luggage being just 5 kg overweight. If I had paid for a heavier bag online, it would have been much cheaper. This is pure exploitation!Overall, I paid nearly €200 for a short flight between Cluj and Basel — and it was one of the most miserable flight experiences I’ve ever had.If you value fair pricing, transparency, and basic comfort, avoid Wizz Air.

If you are looking for the worst airline experience of your life, look no further than Wizz Air. From the moment you book your ticket to the second you (hopefully) land at your destination, this airline will test your patience, your sanity, and your will to live.1. Hidden Fees and Scam PricingWizz Air is the undisputed king of hidden fees. Think you’ve booked a cheap flight? Think again. You’ll pay extra for everything—printing your boarding pass, selecting a seat, bringing a normal-sized carry-on, and even breathing on board (okay, maybe not that last one—yet). Their so-called “low-cost” tickets quickly turn into a price comparable to a much better airline.2. Delays and Cancellations Without ExplanationExpect your flight to be delayed—or even worse, canceled—without a shred of communication. If your flight is canceled, don’t expect any compensation or assistance. You’ll be left stranded in a random airport, begging for information from their unhelpful and rude staff, who will simply tell you to “check the website” while smirking at your misfortune.3. Incompetent and Rude StaffSpeaking of staff, Wizz Air seems to have a strict hiring policy: only employ people who genuinely hate passengers. From the check-in desk to the cabin crew, you’ll be met with indifference, sarcasm, and outright hostility. Have a problem? Too bad. Want a solution? Not happening.4. Cattle-Class Seating and Filthy CabinsThe seats on Wizz Air planes feel like they were designed for medieval torture. Zero legroom, rock-hard cushions, and seats that don’t recline. Oh, and if you thought they cleaned the plane between flights, think again—crumbs, stains, and mysterious sticky substances are all part of the Wizz Air experience.5. A Customer Service Black HoleEver tried contacting Wizz Air’s customer service? Good luck. Their call centers are either nonexistent or staffed by people who couldn’t care less. Emails go unanswered, complaints are ignored, and if you ever get through, they’ll probably just hang up on you.Final Verdict: Avoid at All CostsFlying with Wizz Air is like playing Russian roulette with your travel plans. If you enjoy stress, frustration, and financial scams disguised as budget travel, then go ahead and book with them. Otherwise, save yourself the nightmare and fly literally any other airline.

Denied Boarding – Partial Compensation Only, No Expense ReimbursementI was denied boarding on flight W64565 (Larnaca → Yerevan) on 6 November 2025, despite holding a confirmed booking. The flight was overbooked and operated with a smaller aircraft.Wizz Air offered me €250, although under EU Regulation 261/2004 the correct compensation for denied boarding on a route over 1,500 km is €400. They also refused to reimburse direct expenses (fuel, parking, fast track — total €79), calling them “consequential losses,” which is incorrect in legal terms.They did agree to refund the unused ticket (€139.98), which I appreciate — but overall, their response remains incomplete and not in line with EU law.I’ve filed a formal complaint with the Cyprus Civil Aviation Authority, and will pursue the matter to full resolution.Wizz Air’s refusal to fully comply with passenger rights obligations is disappointing. I hope others will be aware of how they handle such situations.

Beware – This Company Is a ScamI strongly advise against using this company. It’s nearly impossible to reach a live customer service representative by phone, and communication overall is extremely difficult. They also tend to add unexpected charges if you're not paying close attention.We booked a flight from Barcelona to Rome through them and purchased two tickets—one with a 20kg luggage allowance and the other with 26kg. Both suitcases were identical in size. We redistributed our items so that one bag weighed exactly 20kg and the other 24kg.In a rush at the airport and without Wi-Fi access, I didn’t double-check the baggage allowances on our tickets. At check-in, we were charged 60 euros for the second bag supposedly being 4kg over the 20kg limit. After passing security and finally connecting to Wi-Fi, I reviewed our tickets and confirmed that the second bag was actually allowed 26kg.I immediately ran back to the check-in desk. The agent acknowledged the mistake and admitted we were wrongly charged—but said the only way to get a refund was by submitting a claim online.Since returning home, I’ve tried repeatedly to reach someone to resolve the issue—there is no live customer service. I submitted all the necessary documentation by email, and their response was baffling: they rejected my claim, stating the luggage was "oversized."How does that make sense? Both bags were exactly the same size. If the 20kg bag was accepted without issue, how could the identical 24kg bag suddenly be "oversized"?This company lacks common sense and customer support. I strongly urge others to avoid booking with them.

Wizzair deserve Zero. Flight from Leeds Bradford Airport to Gdansk Poland was due to take off at 07:45 on April 1st, it was postponed till 14.30 then 20.20. then 22.20....can you imagine the trust in passengers thinking will this flight won't be postponed again ?To add insult to injury Wizzair did not write clearly on their emails to concerned passengers that the Leeds Airports Luggage Security & Passport  Control on Main Departure Hall  would close early before passengers  are allowed onto departure lounge. An Airline whose communication department can not write proper email advising & warning passengers that Passport security closes just like Gate Departure Closes at A Fixed Time. Hence the chaos.This is what you get when you have incompetent staff in uniforms pretending they know their jobs. This is the sad level of lower standards we live in in 2025 in the most advanced country in the world & supposed to be leader in Aviation Industry Management.The whole passenger experience is a shame to both Leeds Airport & Wizzair. Both incompetent.

I was denied boarding on Wizz Air flight W62221 (Budapest → London Gatwick) on 5 October 2025, even though I had all legally required documents to transit the UK.I hold a Jordanian passport, a valid U.S. Green Card, and had a confirmed onward flight from London to Orlando within 3 hours. According to the official UK government website, U.S. Green Card holders do not require a transit visa when departing within 24 hours. I have taken this exact same route three or four times before without any issues.I showed the Wizz Air/Menzies staff:My passport and Green CardMy onward boarding passUK government rules clearly stating I do not need a visaPrior UK entry stamps from identical tripsDespite acknowledging the information on the UK website, the staff revoked my boarding pass and refused to escalate the matter. As a result, I missed my long-haul flight and had to purchase a last-minute replacement ticket costing over HUF 404,000 (~$1,200).Wizz Air’s later responses to my complaint were generic and did not address the legal facts or explain why I was denied boarding despite being fully eligible to travel. They simply stated their “position remains unchanged” without clarifying what that position is.This incident caused significant financial loss, risk of overstaying my Hungarian residency, and major personal inconvenience. I hope Wizz Air reviews this case seriously and improves staff training on international transit rules to avoid similar wrongful denials in the future.

I did not actually take this flight, I booked it then cancelled it because I had to change my plans. I cancelled it within a few hours on the same day. I did this on the website which clearly stated that from the total cost of £74.65, £21.65 would be deducted as a cancellation fee and that I would be refunded £53 in cash. Several months later, with no refund, I checked my account and saw that £21.65 had been given to me in credits only. The cancelled booking was still there but any attempt to look at the details resulted in an "error". I called the expensive customer service number and was told the opposite of what it had said previously on the website, ie that according to Wizz Air terms and conditions, I had been charged a £53 cancellation fee, that is 71% of the total, for cancelling within a few hours on a then empty flight, in an online transaction which had cost Wizz Air absolutely nothing. Even the £21.65 had not been returned to me in cash as specified. So Wizz Air have in effect taken £74.65 of my money for nothing. I haven't experienced any other airline operating in this way, usually an immediate cancellation will cost £0. While I will be considering legal action, I can say for certain that this will be the last money that Wizz Air make out of me, as I will never be using them again. So from this short-sighted greedy policy, everyone loses.

I asked for a call back from a manager which he said he would request but no one called me back.I then called the next day and spoke to a lady to tell her the whole story again, to which she said fine I will help you request a refund over the phone, she took all of 30 seconds which I thought didn’t seem right but she was adamant she had done it.I call today to check on the state of the refund, to be told it hasn’t even been processed and that it would take 7-10 days to be processed now.It’s funny that I made a compensation claim in line with EU legislation and it was picked up and denied within 24 hours but they won’t pay back a refund that I am entitled to? I can’t explain how poor and unprofessional this company is, you really do get what you pay for sadly!

I booked flights via the Wizz Air app for myself, my wife, and our 4-year-old son. I was tired, and I accidentally selected CRA (Craiova) → CRL (Charleroi) instead of the correct direction: CRL → CRA. It was an honest, human mistake. I noticed it less than one minute after confirming payment.I contacted Wizz Air immediately. But I was faced with a system that:	•	Offers no grace period – not even 5 minutes,	•	Charged me 1,533 RON (~€309) to modify the booking,	•	Or 1,440 RON (~€290) to cancel it,	•	While the total paid for the flight was 1,176 RON (~€237).They rejected my request and replied that I should’ve purchased WIZZ Flex. But I had no intention to cancel or change my plans. I just wanted to correct a booking mistake — and I tried to do so immediately.This route (Charleroi ↔ Craiova) is frequently overbooked. Wizz Air is known to sell more tickets than seats (legally permitted). It’s almost certain that our 3 seats will be resold if we don’t check in.So Wizz Air will lose nothing. On the contrary — they’ll likely profit twice from the same seats.For example Ryanair — known for being strict — gives passengers 24 hours to fix such mistakes for free. Wizz Air? Not even one minute.I submitted a complaint to the European Consumer Centre (ECC Belgium). Their response? They confirmed that Wizz Air is allowed (legally) to refuse any refund — even if the mistake is caught 0.00001 seconds after booking. But even they admitted: “We regret the rigid commercial approach of Wizz Air.”This isn’t about money anymore. It’s about principle:	•	About a system that punishes a harmless error like a crime,	•	About a business that profits not just from tickets, but from your slip-ups,	•	And about a customer experience where empathy has been replaced by algorithms.Mistakes can be forgiven. But only if someone wants to.Triple-check your airport codes if you fly with Wizz Air. Because if you make a mistake, you won’t get a second chance.I didn’t.

This review addresses a deeply distressing and entirely avoidable incident at the check-in desk that highlights a dangerous lack of knowledge among Wizz Air staff regarding international travel regulations.My family, including myself, my wife, and our toddler, were traveling from Mallorca to London. Upon reaching the check-in desk, the agent refused to process our boarding passes based on an absurd and completely incorrect requirement: I was told that, as an EU citizen, I needed to provide proof of residence in the UK to visit.EU citizens are generally permitted to visit the UK for short stays with just a valid passport (or, depending on the current date, an Electronic Travel Authorisation/ETA), and absolutely do not require residency documentation for a tourist visit.The staff member's refusal created a massive and unnecessary delay. As the situation escalated, my toddler began crying uncontrollably, and we were genuinely terrified we would miss our flight due to the desk agent’s fundamental lack of training.We insisted on speaking to a supervisor, but even the supervisor was completely unaware of the correct post-Brexit travel rules for EU citizens visiting the UK. This supervisor was forced to leave the desk and consult with airport Customs officials to verify the rule—a process that should have taken moments but resulted in a significant delay and a growing queue behind us.After a protracted, stressful wait—during which our child was highly distressed and we were subject to the pressure of missing our flight—they finally confirmed what we knew all along: we were compliant with all entry requirements and could proceed.The lack of basic regulatory knowledge displayed by both the desk agent and the supervisor is deeply concerning for an international airline. We demand an explanation for why Wizz Air personnel are not properly trained on crucial immigration requirements, which directly compromised our travel plans and caused extreme, unnecessary emotional distress to a family with a young child. This negligence wasted our time, jeopardized our flight, and reflected poorly on the airline's operational standards.
This is a company to avoid at all costs, it is the worst company I have seen, a unique experience, everything is set up to scam and make people pay more no matter what.

I’m writing publicly because my case remains unresolved after months of emails with both Wizz Air and Loveholidays.I was denied boarding on my confirmed Wizz Air flight from Burgas to Luton on 29 September 2025, even though the flight operated as scheduled. Wizz Air told Loveholidays there were “no seats available”, yet the same flight was still being sold on Wizz Air’s own website at the same time.I had no choice but to buy the same flight again directly from Wizz Air, proving that the original information was false. This cost me additional money, stress, and disruption while stranded abroad.Wizz Air has since refunded part of my booking but is refusing to pay the £700 EC261 compensation for denied boarding and has not explained how this happened. I have also requested my booking reference, associated email, and phone number several times — essential details to submit a claim — but they have never been provided.Both companies have breached passenger rights under UK and EU Regulation 261/2004, the EU Package Travel Directive, and consumer protection laws. I have now given them 14 days to respond before escalating to the CAA, ADR, and Bulgarian Civil Aviation Administration.It’s disappointing that I have to resort to public reviews to get action. I hope this encourages both Wizz Air and Loveholidays to take accountability and resolve this properly.— Erika U.

I've been a loyal Wizz Air customer since their early days, but this experience has been absolutely shocking.What happened:Wizz Air emailed me requesting payment of a specific amount in eurosI transferred the EXACT amount requestedMy flight was cancelled with the warning that my payment was pending and then cancelled.Second booking experiencing the same issueThe problems:Currency scam: After 10+ phone calls and hours on hold, I was told my booking was cancelled due to "currency issues" with their UK bank account. It all went downhill from the moment I tried booking. I booked the flights, but at the last screen this timed out (it took me less than 2 minutes to do, so god knows how fast you actually need to be) and when I went back on, the flights had conveniently increased by over £30.We booked anyway, and originally from Leeds but Wizz changed out flight from the morning to the afternoon, losing 1/2 day of our 3 day trip. We accepted but this was then again changed and this time to 3 days earlier. I was given the option to accept, refund or amend.I amended to flying from London as the flights were more frequent, and when I booked with the new airport I received the confirmation email confirming that it had kept the incorrect date of 3 days earlier. I called the contact centre immediately to amend, and was told it would be £44/person to change (when the tickets were only £78/person anyway). I said I hadn't asked for that date, and through the amendment process it was the correct date; it was only when I got the confirmation email that it had changed again to 3 days earlier. The employee was so rude, to the point of shouting "MADAM MADAM NO SHUT UP" while I was saying literally nothing, and then saying "yes yes very clever very good go away" down the phone when I asked if the calls were recorded as I couldn't believe what I was hearing.I called back and spoke to someone else just as rude but they agreed to refund in full. I got the refund, which was £almost £40 short but simply couldn't be bothered with the hassle so left it. We had already booked and paid for the hotel so were tied to Wizz's flight schedule at this point, and had to rebook the flights.The flight went okay, but they continued to send reminders for the wrong airport and flight dates in the run up to the day which caused a bit of panic.It was £14/person to book seats when checking in early otherwise it was a check in 24 hours prior and the seats would be randomly allocated. We left it to chance, having been given seats at least relatively close together (if not together) with every other airline before, but of course Wizz put our 4 seats in different corners across the plane from each other meaning we had to book them at £14/person to be sat close by.We also chose the £2 uplift for 2 of us for the additional legroom and when we got on the plane, these were normal seats and the second row back rather than the first as shown on the seat map when booking. Since contacting Wizz, they're of course denying these were extra legroom and have no answer as to why those seats were £2 dearer (if not for extra legroom). I'm not spending my days arguing over a £2 uplift with them...... but let it be known, Wizz will do everything possible in their power to scam the odd few quid out of you. Our flights might have been cheap but we're now nearly another £100 down from their mistakes. From timing out your booking, to amending your flights and offering none existent additional legroom, they will find a way to make it as expensive as possible.I wish we had just chosen a well known airline instead, as these bad reviews don't write themselves. If you're reading this considering a flight with Wizz, just don't - don't fall into the trap of "it'll be fine" like we did. Pay the extra and go with someone else.I simply do not know how this shambles of an airline is still running.

I was put on hold and asked to solve this issue at the check in desk at the airport. When we arrived at the airport,  the agent told me that the flight is overbooked and that I have no seat. I was put on standby for 2 hours then was refused a seat with my family. I guess there is no need to go into how distressed and annoyed we all were with this experience and how much my boys cried! Considering that I urgently needed to go back to my family and work as planned and the fact that I hadn’t really planned for an extra 10 days of vacation / expenses, I opted for a compensation (euros 400) and paid myself a one way ticket with BA for Euro 520 for 2 days later. I was told I will be compensated between 4 to 6 weeks. Few weeks later I received an email from customer service asking whether I like a compensation in Wizzair credits or a money transfer. We flew from Vienna to Gatwick and due to Storm Darragh, circled in the air for an hour before landing. Landing was attempted but aborted due to strong winds and we were diverted to Cologne, Germany. Here we sat on the tarmac for 2 hours with minimal information and were still required to pay for water! We were eventually told we were flying back to Vienna. Some passengers chose to get off at Cologne but were told they would not receive compensation if they did. We then took an hours flight back to Vienna. On arrival we were told that ground staff in the airport would rebook our flights and arrange accommodation if needed, and not to do so ourselves or we wouldn't be eligible for compensation. However, we were well and truly DUMPED. We were sent through a side gate of the airport completely bypassing passport control. After collecting baggage and having no idea what to do, a staff member told us to go to arrivals and someone would be with us shortly. 2 HOURS later with no sign of any staff, police show up. Turns out us UK citizens are technically there illegally due to going through the side gate. Once the police had dealt with this, we waited a further 3-4 hours with no food or drink until the airport opened again and staff arrived. Here we were told they couldn't do anything and to book flights and hotels ourselves.To dump 250 passengers in a closed airport with no information, no drinks, no seats or anything was truly DISGUSTING. We are now all fighting for refunds and compensation and struggling to get anywhere. When things go wrong, WizzAir do not care at all about their passengers!

On our recent family trip to Venice we have experienced some technical issues when trying to do our online check-in. This has caused a lot of stress and i ended up paying for extra services. On the way to Venice we had no issues doing the online check-in, but on the way back it was impossible to check in online. I have double checked my email and the booking details to make sure it was not a mistake from my side, i have even tried to do the online check-in from a different device such as a tablet, a laptop, another phone but it was still unsuccessful. No matter what we tried we just couldn't finish the check in. At the section with the passenger passport details i could fill in everyone's information except for my daughter's details. The issues was that it wouldn't allow me to select her date of birth, the year wouldn't go higher than 2010. I've downloaded the wizz air app thinking that it would help. I never experienced this type of problem before and i didn't know what to do. I've tried to contact wizz air customer service but that was also impossible . The chat box assistant and the customer service number was unavailable. We also tried to scan her passport rather than putting her details manually and that as well gave error saying that the passport is invalid. Once desperate, i decided to pay for the autocheck-in service not knowing that once i pay, i will still have the same issue. As a last option i decided to pay for the airport check-in service but that was again useless, as i still had to fill in the passport details and was still not able to select the correct birth year. At first, I believed that maybe it was because when we went to Venice my daughter was 11 years old and on our way back from Venice back home she had already turned 12. But that couldn't have been the case since that goes automatically when you have to select the child's age and fill in the personal details the moment you do the booking for the flight. Also, whenever i purchased the extra services such as autocheck-in and airport check-in, i had to pay for the whole family, even though the problem was only for one of them. I ended up spending extra £££ due to some technical issues, and that had nothing to do with my booking or my daughter's passport. Luckily, when we arrived at the airport the lady at the check in desk she was able to help us check-in. When I wrote in to try to get back the money that I payed extra, their response was like a copy-paste  version of their "helping customer guide" which gave me the feeling that my email wasn't even read fully. Basically I paid extra because of their technical issue and never got a refund from them, or at least a decent response. I even ended up calling them on their 800 number (which is not cheap) and still for nothing. This company is a complete JOKE. To top it up, on our way back from Venice to London I was charged extra for my luggage ( the same luggage we went with from London to Venice and it was all good then) . Their excuse was that my bag was to big and it was supposed to be checked in. I was even told that there is no space on the plane for my bag anyhow, that the plane was full. When boarding the plane I realised that there were more than 30 empty spaces. Im guessing that because they didn't made enough sales for their flight they started charging passengers for their bags,... they had to get their money from somewhere. I was definitely not the only one that was charged for their bags that day. A bunch of sneaky, rude and unprofessional, that have no consideration for parents with small children, pregnant women or anyone else.Was definitely my last time flying with them, ill choose Ryanair for EasyJet instead.

I recently endured a harrowing journey with Wizz Air from Baku to Abu Dhabi. The flight was plagued by delays, with little to no communication from the airline. This is especially problematic for those who may not have access to their phones or may have unreliable internet connections.Despite Wizz Air's own policy of providing adequate food and water during delays, the options offered were utterly insufficient. As a vegetarian, I was only offered a hot dog and water. This is unacceptable, especially considering European Regulation 261/2004 requires airlines to provide appropriate food and drink during delays.Wizz Air's customer service is equally as appalling. The only way to file a complaint is through their predetermined form, which offers no room for adequate explanation or context. When I inquired about other channels for complaints, such as social media or email, I was informed that there is no such option. This is a blatant disregard for customer welfare.I strongly advise anyone considering flying with Wizz Air to reconsider. Giving 1 star because there is no option to give a 0 star.My husband and I had a very bad experience with WizzAir on what it was supposed to be our flight from Rome to Paris. It was an absolute nightmare from start to finish. The level of disorganisation and lack of professionalism was staggering.We arrived at Rome airport more than 3 hours before our scheduled flight to Paris, only to find ourselves stuck in an incredibly long, chaotic check-in line. There were hundreds of people in the same line, all going to different destinations, which made everything even more confusing and disorganised.Despite patiently waiting in line for over two full hours, once we finally reached the counter, the check-in staff coldly informed us that our flight had already closed and there was nothing they could do. We explained that we had been in line the entire time—along with others heading to the same destination—but their only response was that we would not be allowed to check our luggage and therefore not able to board.Even more heartbreaking, there was another family in line with us, travelling with a small baby, who were also denied boarding after waiting just as long. It was clear that this wasn’t an isolated issue—it was a systemic failure in how WizzAir manages their flights and passengers.No clear announcements. No separate lines for different flights. No staff coordinating or informing people. Just pure chaos and a total lack of accountability. When we asked for help or options, we were told bluntly that they couldn’t do anything. No apology, no rebooking support, nothing. The manager was rude and we felt like they are so used to the chaos that didn’t even care about our complaints.WizzAir’s incompetence caused us and the other family (with a small baby) to miss our flight, waste our time, and suffer unnecessary stress—and they refused to take any responsibility for it.I strongly recommend avoiding this airline unless you want to gamble your travel plans on a company that clearly doesn’t care about its passengers or basic organisation. Not even even in the same 30mins did the prices go down. Naturally, I wanted to get the better price. I called their customer service and after 40 mins of waiting, the agent hung up the call in the middle of speaking with me. I received a customer feedback email to rate my call where all the links were broken, so no feedback... I then tried to cancel my more expensive booking both online and on the app, which threw up an error "Booking not found" despite being on a page where my booking was listed. i checked for a complaints form or email address. None available, other than a claims form which has very specific categories, none of which applied to me. I called the customer service number again and again, after 20 minutes, spoke to an agent who clearly has very limited options available to him. I explained that I want the lower price available amd he said that he can process a cancellation but, not only will I not get a refund, but the cancellation fee turned out to actually be almost double the cost of the ticket! I had a disappointing experience with Wizz Air during an international flight from Antalya Airport with my two young children (aged 1 and 4). This has been the worst customer experience I have ever had.I had to cancel my flight back in August after Wizz Air changed the timetable twice, with no possibility to reschedule because all flights were fully booked.I submitted a claim explaining that the credit card I originally used was no longer active, as the account had been deleted. Wizz Air and their agent at Gatwick is either incompetent or dishonest. We booked to fly from Gatwick Airport to Pdogoricar on the 05.30, queued for 2 hours at the shambles of a checkin, ran to the gate where our luggage had been offloaded on the false claim that my wife’s Hong Kong passport didn't allow her to fly to Monetenegro. At embarkation on the flight W4 3081 from Bucharest to Brussels at 12.10, on Wednesday the 16th of July, at the Otopeni Airport, Bucharest, a staff picked us from the queue to ask us to pay additionally, for each ticket 78 euro because they said, our luggage is not compliant.Yet, each was standard flight luggage, 40x30x20 without the handle and rolls, exactly as specified on the Wizzair site. It also fitted vertically, as the painted arrow indicated, in their frame at the gate and under the seat since we used it before.They stated, to our surprise, that the luggage had to be of backpack type as in the picture on our ticket only and fit horizontally in their check frame. Yet, on the Wizzair site, it is clearly stated that these pictures are only for illustration.As we were not allowed to embark until last, we watched everybody else boarding  - and were watched standing sanctioned at the gate -  we noticed that many bags were not backpacks at all and few could fit in the frame but none was checked or stopped. When we remarked that, they called us "carcotasi", "grumblers" to our face. That felt so degrading by now.It was clearly a disconnect between the Wizzair gate staff's view and the official luggage specification of Wizzair. Yet, even when we referred the staff to own site, they refused to consult it, arguing that they know better... because they work there.They even argued that the roller luggage is only permitted for priority as in the picture on tickets  but those rollers are much larger, 55x40x30.So, we had to pay 154 euro for a gross airline mistake, we got a dress down in public which upset us terribly, were even admonished by some passengers there, because Wizzair staff didn't know their stuff.Had their staff been flexible enough to check own site and skilled and trained enough to work with the public, this would not have happenedThey even refused to identify themselves and turned their badges backwards to block us.As another surprising fact for us, on our flight, even though there were seats available, one right next to me, at the online checkin our two seats were allocated at random, far apart. It looks like a sanction for those like us in the economy, so that next time they pay more.

When flying out from Barcelona El Prat Airport, I was made pay 48 Euros for check-in at the airport. The booking code they sent to me via email was invalid as it contained six letters. They also turned me away when I asked for assistance the night before the flight at the airport.Also, when flying to Barcelona, I had to try to check-in online, because they sent me two emails telling me to do so.Therefore, I tried two times, the process was different each time. At the first attempt, the company wanted me to create an account. At the second attempt, they told me that I had booked check-in at the airport. So, no need to check in online. When flying out of Barcelona, the company insisted that I only had online-check-in in the booking. They behaved really unhelpful, to say the least.When I told them that I was going to rate them on Trustpilot, they said that this was a personal threat and they would exclude me from the flight.When I asked for an aisle seat, they said, the seating was completely random and that they could not change it. In the end, there were several free seats on the aisle on the aeroplane. So, sitting there was no problem in the end.Be aware of hidden fees and completely chaotic pricing without any logic. This pertains to your check-in process, how much baggage you can bring along, what choosing your seat will cost, and more. In the chaos, do not lose your clear view of your entire booking.However, I would not recommend anyone to book with them at all. And certainly not using a third platform when doing so. The data might not be transmitted correctly. And they use this as an excuse.They also used as an excuse that my surname, which, officially, includes a title, caused the booking problem. The check-in agent unilaterally changed our paid seats (£76) without permission, falsely citing a rule about lap infants needing to sit by the window. I am extremely disappointed with my recent experience with Wizz Air.I paid for baggage in advance when purchasing my tickets, and the total cost for 4 flight tickets with two bags was €390. However, at the airport I was forced to pay an additional €144 for the same two bags — effectively paying for baggage twice.The explanation given was that although I had purchased 10 kg baggage, I had not “checked it in.” This is extremely confusing and was never clearly communicated. I had already completed online check-in, which reasonably suggests that all travel requirements — including baggage — are in order.This raises a serious usability issue:👉 What is the difference between “checked-in passengers” and “checked-in luggage,” especially when the luggage is standard cabin size?At no point during booking or check-in was it clearly stated that:a 10 kg bag (even cabin-sized) must be physically checked infailing to do so would result in a forced and expensive tariff change at the airportInstead, this crucial information appears to be hidden in fine print or secondary links.As a result:I paid for baggage in advanceI was charged again at the airport (€144)the total cost became completely disproportionate to the original ticket priceThis is a clear example of hidden costs / drip pricing, where essential conditions are not transparently disclosed upfront, leading to unexpected charges under pressure at the airport.Customer support did not address the actual issue and ignored my follow-up complaint.This is not just poor service — it is misleading and non-transparent pricing design.I strongly advise other passengers to be extremely cautious, as the price you initially pay may not reflect what you will ultimately be charged.

I was denied boarding on flight W4 6014 from Madrid Adolfo Suárez Airport to Rome Fiumicino on 17 May 2026, scheduled to depart at 20:35.I had valid travel documentation, completed check-in correctly, and arrived at the boarding gate on time.During boarding, I was informed that I would need to pay an additional fee for my backpack. As I had previously travelled several times with Wizz Air using the same backpack without any issue, and was confident that it fitted under the seat, I explained this to the boarding staff member and then joined the boarding queue.At that point, the staff member approached me and stated that she was going to call security because I had entered the queue “without permission. Although I disagreed with the charge, I immediately replied that I was willing to pay for the backpack and asked her to tell me the amount I needed to pay in order to board the flight and avoid any conflict..However, despite my repeated attempts to resolve the situation cooperatively and my clear willingness to pay the requested fee, she insisted that “the damage had already been done as I had joined the queue” and continued with her decision to call security rather than allowing me to complete payment and board the aircraft.During this interaction, the female staff member — a young woman with shoulder-length dark hair, dark eyes, and burgundy glasses — accused me of attempting to “bribe” her, despite the fact that I was simply asking to pay the luggage fee she herself had requested.She also stated that she could report the matter to the police and that I could be fined. I calmly explained that I did not understand the basis for such threats, as I was not acting violently, causing any disturbance, or creating discomfort for staff or passengers. My only intention was to pay the requested fee and travel on the flight, even though I did not believe the charge was reasonable, as the backpack fitted perfectly under the seat and I had previously travelled with the same bag on Wizz Air flights without issue.Despite my cooperative attitude throughout the interaction, the situation was unnecessarily escalated and I was ultimately denied boarding. I asked the Wizz Air staff to provide me with a written document stating the reason why I was being denied boarding, but they refused to provide one. Following this, security personnel were called. Security personnel were called and I was escorted out of the airport. I also asked the Wizz Air employee who denied me boarding and the Security employee who took me out of the airport for their names and surnames, and they stated that they were not obliged to provide me with that information.As a direct consequence, I missed my flight and was forced to purchase a replacement ticket with Ryanair departing later that evening at 22:15, at a personal cost of €189, in order to return to Rome and attend work the following morning. In addition, the replacement flight was delayed by approximately one hour, causing further inconvenience and resulting in my arrival home at around 03:30 a.m.

Mulți pasageri acceptă 100€ fără să știe că sunt păcăliți.Recomand tuturor să refuze aceste „oferte”, să ceară totul în scris și să reclame compania. Este rușinos ca o companie aeriană europeană să își trateze clienții cu atâta lipsă de transparență.Wizz Air, îmbunătățiți-vă politicile și respectați drepturile pasagerilor!We were placed on standby by Wizz Air even though we had valid check-in, EU passports, and tickets purchased in February. The airline offered us €100 to give up our seats, without informing us that we were legally entitled to €400 per person under EU Regulation 261/2004 for involuntary denied boarding.This is a misleading and unfair practice. Many passengers unknowingly accept the €100 offer and get tricked out of their full compensation.I strongly advise all passengers to refuse such offers, request everything in writing, and file complaints.Wizz Air, respect your passengers and stop exploiting legal ignorance. Shameful behavior for a European airline.

Subject: Formal Complaint Regarding Unprofessional and Humiliating Behaviour – Flight W4 6203 (09 December)Dear Wizz Air Customer Relations Team,My name is Seymur Asadov, and I was a passenger on flight W4 6203 from Rome (Fiumicino) to Baku on 09 December, seat 2A.I am submitting a formal complaint regarding extremely unprofessional, disrespectful and humiliating behaviour from one of your flight attendants.Shortly after I visited the lavatory (only to wash my hands), a female flight attendant approached me in front of other passengers, pointed her finger at me, and accused me of not flushing the toilet.This accusation was completely false. Her tone and manner were rude, disrespectful, and caused unnecessary public embarrassment.During the flight, I observed more than 15 other passengers using the lavatory without flushing, yet she said nothing to any of them. When I politely asked why I was singled out, she refused to answer.Furthermore, the flight attendant was not wearing a name badge for the entire duration of the flight, which violates mandatory identification rules.After I asked several times for her name, she finally said her name was “Diana”, but refused to show any official identification.At the end of the confrontation, when I told her that I would file a complaint, she responded with a smirk and said something along the lines of:“Will you go there and apologize?”Her expression and attitude were mocking and provocative.This behaviour felt deeply humiliating and inappropriate. I travel with Wizz Air frequently, and I have never submitted a complaint before. I do not seek any compensation; however, this incident was serious enough to make me reconsider flying with Wizz Air again. I believe passengers deserve respectful and professional treatment, and I intend to report this incident to all relevant aviation authorities as well.I kindly request:1. A full internal investigation into this crew member’s behaviour.2. Confirmation whether a flight attendant named “Diana” was part of the crew.3. Clarification regarding the absence of a mandatory name badge.4. Information about disciplinary and corrective measures that will be taken.5. A written response to this complaint.Thank you for your attention. I expect Wizz Air to handle this incident with the seriousness it requires.Kind regards,Seymur AsadovSeat: 2AFlight W4 6203 (09 December)

Excessive and unjustified charge of €192, far above the standard fees normally charged for airport check-in (typically between €45–€50).2. Technical failure of the app to allow free check-in, despite being well within the required check-in timeframe.3. Lack of clear communication and transparency by Wizz Air, which misleads passengers and creates confusion.4. Evidence of recurring abuse, as thousands of other passengers have reported similar experiences online.Requests:Full refund of the €192 charged under these unfair and misleading circumstances;Immediate review of the company's check-in procedures and mobile app functionality to ensure clear and fair access to free check-in;Public clarification and accountability for the high number of similar cases;Compensation for the stress, inconvenience, and poor customer experience, and a formal response within a reasonable timeframe.Flight Information:Flight Number: W46061 (sequence 197)Booking Confirmation: ZE1DNEDate: July 17, 2025From: Rome Fiumicino (FCO)To: Paris Orly (ORY)Departure Time: 06:15 AMArrival at Airport: 03:15 AM (three hours in advance)Attachments: Payment receipt, app screenshots, check-in attempt records, and any relevant supporting documents.If this issue is not resolved adequately, I reserve the right to escalate this complaint to aviation regulatory authorities (such as ENAC) and European consumer protection bodies.I look forward to your prompt response and a fair resolution to this matter.Sincerely,Rogerio Gagliardi Data do envio: 17/07/25
