I am extremely disappointed with Wizz Air’s handling of my case (PNR NHK4HV). From the beginning, their managers have misled me and avoided responsibility.First, manager Danila provided me with a false reason for my flight cancellation. Since I work in aviation, it was easy for me to recognize that his explanation was fictitious. Later, another manager, Boris, admitted that Danila’s explanation was indeed incorrect. However, Boris also refused to provide the real reason for the cancellation, since this information would confirm my right to €250 compensation under EU law.Then, a third manager, Anton, took over my case. Instead of solving the issue, he closed my ticket, saying Wizz Air would handle the matter with my legal team. At the same time, the airline told my legal team their ticket was closed because Wizz Air would only deal with me. This contradictory communication shows Wizz Air is deliberately avoiding responsibility.The disruption also forced me to change my travel plans completely. Instead of flying from Zakynthos to Vienna as booked, I was rerouted to Budapest, which caused significant extra costs. The next day, when I arrived at check-in for the rebooked flight, the manager at the desk informed me that, due to overbooking, I was removed from the flight. Only after I insisted on speaking with the airport manager and explained that Wizz Air had already canceled my flight once and now was removing me again, was I finally allowed to board.Three different managers, three different stories, no transparency, no accountability, and enormous stress and costs on my side. It is clear that Wizz Air does not want to provide fair service or comply with EU passenger rights. Closing tickets without reason, refusing to provide the true cause of the cancellation, and nearly denying me boarding again demonstrates incompetence and unprofessionalism.I demand that Wizz Air reopens my case, provides the real reason for the cancellation in writing, and pays the  compensation as required by law. This review is public because I have no other way to be heard. I hope Wizz Air finally takes responsibility and contacts me directly.

AVOID IF POSSIBLE I have flown with several low-cost airlines (e.g., EasyJet, Ryanair, Eurowings, Vueling, etc.), and I understand that their business model revolves around secondary services and upsells. The airline’s lack of transparency, particularly regarding service fees, seems like an open attempt to trap passengers with excessive hidden charges that often double the price of the trip.For example, I received an email on the day of my flight stating, "Check-in is available 2.5 hours before departure, and up to 1 hour prior." What it failed to mention is that checking in during this period incurs an additional airport fee. This omission feels deliberate. I’m not alone in this; during my experience, I saw five other passengers—three in a group and a couple—who were caught off guard by the same charge.If this airline wants to improve its reputation (which is already questionable, based on reviews), it must address customer complaints seriously and be upfront about all fees. Until then, I hope the European Commission for Consumer Rights takes note of this practice and holds the company accountable.Customers should not accept such blatant exploitation.

So, at the end, I was free to leave that nightmare. I paid for a hotel and a flight back to London the following day with a different airline myself. I contacted the airline to ask for compensation, as they never delivered the service sold and they put everybody's safety as risk. Review: recruitment process in Gatwick UK Marriot Hotel WizzAir 7.03.25 Wizzair is a completely unfair, illogical, and disappointed recruitment process. Saw advertising on Indeed WizzAir recruiting flight attendant, it wasn't any particular requirement needed, I was excited, then invited for recruitment. I was so happy after months applying for jobs. WizzAir back to me in few days. Then I received a call from them, lady was ones again invited me to interview, confirming me that all training will be given. One things is not allowed, she told me, its tattoo and piercing.Came with big hope 7.03.25, went through a few stages, and passed it. But stress was always in the air for many people.After passing a few stages, WizzAir representative told us how it would look like to work with WizzAir - it all looked and sounds great. Contract would be 18 months and then ongoing. You can even choose a location if you want to relocate. Many candidates were excited.  I thought i got it, and the lady interviewer asked me which location I would prefer, Luton or Gatwick, I confirmed Gatwick. I was happy again. Cherry, on the top was the pre last stage, called:  role play:  means resolving issues with difficult passengers  - to see how candidates will resolve the issue which could potentially happen in airport or on the plane. That was a surprise.WizzAir asked us to demonstrate it, however non of us have been ever trained for it. Training is supposed to be later, after they recruit you. However task needed to be performed on same day, to go through to the next stage.I sow how many candidates went upset after this "task " to deal with issues on the plane or in airports with difficult passengers. None of us have never been trained for it. I was shocked by it. It's like to be dentist, without having proper training.My task was: angry passenger was mad that he needed to pay for his bag. Angry passenger was member of WizzAir recruitment team. It was like a theatre performens. My role was to calm him down, and I did it. But yet again, I had never been trained for it and just naturally pulled all I could. They however told me it wasn't good enoughI felt  that it all wrong,  that I wasn't sure what exactly I should be doing, neither what the correct way to deal with this task. My hands were shaking and i went into total distress in the room.The other lady had  some meltdown, when she was told that she didn't perform as well as needed, despite one's again non of us never dealt with anything like this, and wasn't ready.We are supposed to be trained for 4 weeks, then pass exams, and only after dealing with the passengers issues. Not on the first day of recruitment. It's illogical. What is the boundary in aviation industry for customer service? I had no idea. It's a different customer service in different industry. A few minutes later, me and other candidates were told that we all failed to perform. Yet again, none of us have been trained for it or ready for it. I couldn't believe in it. We been rejected for the things none of us knew how to deal with it. We been told we can only reapply for same job in 90 days. Only properly trained people can resolve an issue with passengers, not before training.  Even security job need training, and get qualified for it.We been asked to do so, and felt that it's way too much to ask in the first day.Wizzair never mentioned what to expect from this interview, no brief information, no mention to perform something like this.  Disappointed, and I felt it was total waste of time and misleading selective process from WizzAir, lacking clarity, I felt it was breach of all possible recruitment process and rules, where potential candidates left in total shock, after recruitment experience.Also going through the stages is quite exhausting, you could potentially sit and wait from 9am to 6 pm. It depends how lucky you are. From my experience I feel I was unfairly treated so as many other candidates who left disappointed.

Difficult to describe how much I hate this company. Every time I promise myself I won't be using Wizzair again but then after a couple of years I forget.This company today overbooked my flight by 11 people. 11 souls wasted many hours in the airport today to finally be sent home because the flight that they booked never actually had seat for them.. By law, they will have to compensate more than 3k (250 each plus hotels and transport) total and obviously deal with the complaints.The lady at the desk literally told me that it was my fault for: A. booking with a low cost airline; B. not doing the check in between 24 and 22 hours before the departure. Complaint Regarding Handbag Issue and Poor Service Experience on Flight 7018Dear Wizz Air Customer Service,I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to express my deep dissatisfaction with the service I received during my recent travel with Wizz Air. Today, on flight 7018 from Alexandria to Abu Dhabi, I encountered an issue regarding my handbag at the airport, which was handled in a very unprofessional and disappointing manner by the desk attendant.The attendant was unhelpful and exhibited a poor attitude when I raised my concerns. Instead of offering assistance, they responded with hostility and a lack of understanding, which only added to my frustration. This is not the standard of service I expect from your airline, and certainly not how customers should be treated.I was informed that my bag weighed about 10 kg and needed to be reduced to 7 kg. I complied and made the necessary adjustments to bring the weight down to 7 kg. However, despite meeting the weight requirement, the attendant still refused to allow me to take the bag on board without providing any valid reason. After contacting some friends with connections at the airport, I was informed that I should have been allowed to take the bag onboard without any issue. This inconsistency in enforcing policies raises serious concerns.I kindly request that Wizz Air address this matter promptly, clarify the handbag policy, and investigate the behavior of the staff at the desk. This kind of experience should not be repeated for other passengers.I look forward to your prompt response and a resolution to this issue.Thank you for your attention to this matter.

The worst service. If you have any quiet you will hold for half hour and the phone will keep cutting off. If you get through to someone they cannot hear you and will cut off. The online chat doesn’t open. You cannot email or ask any questions. My is issue is left unresolved and my flight is in two weeks.
You’ll be left in the airplane for two hours without AC in a very hot country and forced to pay for water to survive and never receive any compensation or even apology

Fight Rome - Bucarest they were leaving me on stand by, then I got lucky but other people were left behind. The steward told me they always do overboking . The flight Bucarest- Rome departed with 3 hours delay, but they didn't get me any compensation as they arrived with less than 3 hours delay. I will never fly with them again.

Terrible experience with WizzAir – I will never fly with them again.My return flight from Spain was cancelled after a power outage at the airport. When I submitted a claim for my additional costs (with receipts), they promised reimbursement – and then ignored me, rejected my follow-up claims, and closed the case without reason.Unprofessional, careless, and disrespectful to passengers. Worst airline

Unprofessional and disrespectful boarding gate staff. On the early morning flight from Gatwick to Malaga on the 5th of November, I queued to board and I found the woman's (I cannot call her lady), at the boarding gate check in, attitude was sharp, impolite and racist. She was not friendly to other than the English! Her attitude after checking my British passport was "go and sit there". I do not know what her background is and how she was brought up! Maybe she does not know to talk normal to people and how to respect others! Very bad for Wizz air  to recruit such people. I fly frequently with Wizz air and they are great, but need to pay attention who they recruit for serving their customers. Never came across such rude staff with any other airlines!

The worst airline company ever! Unhelpful staff. Forced us to pay for check in at the airport when chek in online not available. 2x We flew from Luton to Split Croatia. Rude, non cooperative staff at the gate. (Blonde old lady)
Worst air company ever. No competition

I bought the “All You Can Fly” ticket, then requested a refund after three weeks (which is compliant with their 30-day refund policy).The reason is that in over three weeks, I didn’t manage to book even a single flight!I’m still waiting for a refund after MONTHS! I booked return flights for my husband and I through booking.com. Wizz air cancelled the return  flights. I contacted booking.com to get a refund for these cancelled flights. They processed the request. I am still waiting for wizz air to give me my refund. I will never book through this company again.

Scummy airline that will play any trick to scam people out of money. Charged me €45 to print a piece of paper boarding ticket. Cost more than the flight. Raised it but they didn't care. Customer support is the worst. Asked them to delete all my data asI won't be using them again. Good luck with customer retention, scum bags.

My flight which I had booked vi trip.com has been changed twice in two months and after claiming the refund they say it is not refundable. That’s not fair!!! It was not me changing the flight twice!!! This is my last time using trip.com and wizzair.👎🏼👎🏼👎🏼👎🏼👎🏼👎🏼

I booked a flight AND car rental package via Wizz Air and paid the full amount directly to them. The car rental was clearly included in my booking confirmation, and the amount was deducted from my account.A few days later, I received an email from the car rental provider stating the payment had not been received and that my reservation would be cancelled unless I paid again. I immediately contacted Wizz Air, sent evidence, and asked them to sort it out.They ignored it.The car rental was cancelled due to non-payment — despite the fact that Wizz Air had already charged me. When I followed up again with full evidence (bank statement, cancellation notice), they had the audacity to tell me the booking didn’t include a car rental and that it’s “not their responsibility” because it was a partner service.This is outright fraud.They took my money, failed to deliver the service, refused to refund me, and closed the complaint. I had to rebook a last-minute car rental myself and pay significantly more.Wizz Air hides behind “partner companies” when things go wrong — even when YOU PAY THEM DIRECTLY. Their customer service is robotic, careless, and refuses to acknowledge any responsibility.I will never use Wizz Air again and strongly advise others to avoid booking anything. Their “services” are a scam. If anything goes wrong, you’re on your own.

Worst airline ever. You think you’re getting a good deal, but they charge extra for absolutely everything, so it ends up costing far more than expected. We were also charged an extra £90 for not checking in online, even though the app wouldn’t allow us to check in. To make matters worse, when we arrived in Budapest our suitcase was damaged and missing a wheel. Avoid this airline.

Avoid at all costs, you will end up paying double the cost of a ticket for check in, changing or any other services. On top of that, staff are very rude and unprofessional. My worse experience of any airline has been Wizz Air!

Worst airline I've ever had to fly with. I wish I had a different option to get to Sibiu because I would never recommend flying with Wizz Air, it's awful. I've flown with them many times and each time I encountered major issues. Just to name a few: cancelled or extremely delayed flights, gross overbooking or luggage issues. Basically, unless you pay for a seat, your space on the plane is not guaranteed and there is always a possiblity for you to be left out, even after you've paid hundreds of pounds for a ticket. Also, be careful of their deceptive baggage rules that they keep changing. We had to pay £68 at the gate for a trolley bag because I didn't realise they had changed their rules regarding carry on luggage and this was somehow only applied to one trip and not the return trip as well. It was my mistake not to check before travelling, but never again. Check their rules every time you fly with them because you never know when they change them.On top of that, the planes themselves are awful, there's little to no legroom, the seats are uncomfortable and there's not even a charging point available on board. Also, don't be fooled by the "low cost" promises they make because every chance they get they will rip you off and make you pay extra for basic amenities. Rather go with a more "expensive" airline and don't waste your time with this joke of a company.

If you're after customer service from these guys then forget about it. 29th August at Bucharest waiting for the 19:40 to Luton. We were herded to gate 38. Flight was delayed. No info given for reason for delay. 2 ignorant members of gate staff were unable and unwilling to help despite being begged for an explanation. Wizz app showed no reason for delay. The chat on the app showed no reason for delay. There was tall of no bus being available to take us to the aircraft. About an hour after our scheduled departure, we were bussed to the aircraft. I received the following at 20.47. Flight 3007 OTP-LTN has been delayed, new departure time: 20:40, 8/29/25Check-in closes as per the original schedule. Please see email for details.WIZZ   Staff on aircraft blamed the airshow for the delay Customer service via X at best totally clueless with even a simple explanation. I've withdrawn my complaint as too much time already wasted. Never again.

I’d heard mixed reviews about Wizz Air, but I assumed it couldn’t be that bad. Unfortunately, my experience proved otherwise.We tried to check in online well within the allocated time, but the system was completely down. We spent hours on and off attempting to check in, taking time-stamped screenshots as proof of the issue. When we arrived at the airport and explained this to the check-in staff, we expected some understanding. Instead, we were immediately told we had to pay €97 to check in at the counter.Despite showing them clear evidence that their system wasn’t working, they refused to acknowledge the issue or offer any help. Later, another staff member admitted that Wizz Air is facing legal action over this exact problem—blocking online check-in to charge passengers extra. We weren’t the only ones on our flight affected by this.It’s disappointing to see an airline operate this way. This experience has put me off flying with Wizz Air again, and I’d advise others to be aware of these tactics before booking.

I am extremely disappointed with the way my recent flight experience was handled. My travel was severely disrupted due to poor communication and operational failure on your part.We were instructed to disembark the aircraft and were told to wait for a replacement flight. We complied, expecting proper updates and assistance. However, no announcement was made, no updated boarding time was shown on the screens, and no communication from your staff was provided.To our shock, we later discovered that the original flight had departed without us. No one informed us of this change or gave us a chance to re-board. As a result, I was forced to book a new flight at my own expense and paid an additional $500.This is unacceptable. The complete lack of information and support from your staff led to unnecessary stress, financial loss, and a ruined travel plan.

Never, ever book a flight with this company!I've been denied boarding twice in my entire life — and both times were with Wizzair. Guess how many times I've booked a Wizzair flight? Exactly two.The first time was in London. I arrived at the gate nearly 25 minutes before departure, saw the open door of the aircraft, and could clearly see into the cabin — yet they insisted the gate was closed.The second (and last) time, I was sitting right in front of the gate almost an hour before departure, calmly reading my book. When the gate opened, passengers began queuing just a few meters from me. As usual, I waited for the line to clear before approaching. It couldn’t have been more than a minute or two after the last person passed through when I got up — only to be told the gate was already closed. I was stunned. The bus transporting passengers to the plane was still right there with its doors open. I pleaded with the gate agent, pointing out that it was still 20 minutes before departure and the bus was clearly still boarding. She either refused to answer or just repeated “no.”Now, after reading reviews from others, I realize this is most likely due to overbooking — which, from what I’ve seen, seems to be a deliberate strategy on Wizzair’s part. So shameful, so disgusting!

They debited my credit card £163.84 completely without my consent and for no reason. They have finally refunded me £101.52 when I complained but this leaves a balance of £62.32 which to date they have not repaid to me. They totally ignore my repeated complaints to Customer Service.

In the past month, my family and I flew London to Athens and back twice.  The first time to visit my mother in hospital, and the second to attend her funeral.  The second flight back to the UK was with Wizzair, whom we had not used before.The night before the flight I checked my email to see whether I had received a check-in email, but I had not, so I assumed they wanted us to check-in in at the airport.  The next morning, we were busy with packing, goodbyes, and catching public transport for our long journey to the airport.  However, Wizzair did send a check-in email that day (I have never before received a check-in email as late as this!)When we got to the airport, we were told that we had to pay a charge for not checking in on-line.  I thought this was fair, but rather expensive at €48.  Then the thunderbolt struck: this was PER PERSON for a 5 member family!!!The check-in member of staff was very apologetic, but she did not apparently work for Wizzair.  The person at the payment desk had the attitude of "oh no, another poor victim of Wizzair”.  Well, I had no choice but to pay but it felt like being robbed or blackmailed.And what did we get for our check-in, supposedly worth almost €250?We got to all sit separately!But this was only the beginning:- I got to sit next to a rude, talkative, obese person.  This turned out to be not such a big problem seeing as I had to spend most of the flight checking up on my children! I basically stayed away from my seat except for take-off and landing!- The plane was delayed because there was too much cabin luggage for the space, and they took a long time to fit it all in.- The flight was scheduled to take half an hour longer than the other flights we took in these two trips (Why? Does the Wizzair pilot not push the throttle as much as others? Are they saving on fuel or something?)- The plane was generally crowded and smelled of urine.In short, this was a nightmare of a journey, and certainly the worst flight I have ever experienced.I was told by several people that this is what happens with "these budget airlines", yet I have always flown with budget airlines and never had something that bad, shocking, and insulting, and at a difficult time of my life.  And the final "joke"?  Out of the four flights, this was the most expensive even excluding the absurd check-in charge!!

No 24h free flight cancellation/change option. I immediately saw that the ticket I bought was for the wrong flight, but the cancellation fee was more expensive than the flights itself. I had to change the flight otherwise they'll charge €80 per flight for not showing up. :)))))That cost me additional €84. Avoid WizzAir if possible. Message to other airlines: Check wizzair's routes and have one there as well. They charge extremely high because competition is non-existent or low.

Awful awful airline. Avoid at all costs! Waited 3 hours for delayed flight at Naples airport with no update,  to finally be ushered onto the plane. Sat on the plane for over an hour before being asked to go back into the airport with no explanation.. eventually fellow passengers found out by googling (!!) that Wizz air had decided the flight staff weren’t allowed to work on the flight..  wouldn’t anyone have thought to check this first?!! Sat in airport for further 2 hours with NO communication and not a person in sight who resembled customer service. When we finally found someone they had no idea and blamed other Wizz staff..Eventually we were ushered into a bus and put up in a hotel at 10pm to be told we had to be up at 330am for a rescheduled flight.. which itself was delayed!  Shambles of an airline, I agree with many of the other comments on here.. use another airline if you have any option..We have been told we are entitled compensation however Wizz air have absolved themselves from any responsibility blaming the 3rd party we booked through despite having been advised Wizz air being legally responsible. Joke of a company

They seize every opportunity to deceive passengers with subtle tricks and pick their pockets. They are greedy in every detail of the booking, check-in, and flight experience. In return, they deliver rudeness and contempt, treating us like cattle.

4th October, 17:30, Luton airport. The worst customer service I ever experienced in my whole life. Whizz's supervisor, a white lady with black hair and blue eyes, was rude and threatened my husband and me. It was our first time travelling by Whizz, and we couldn't do the check-in online. So we decided to ask for help at the airport, 3 hours before the plane departed. We said, "Hello, how are you today?" but she was unable to look at us, say good afternoon back, and rudely told us, "Next time I'll charge £45 each if you don't check in online."I was in shock, and the CCTV for sure registered that. I used to work with customer service, and this behaviour is absolutely unacceptable. If she is not happy with her job, she must quit and find another one. But never, never treat a customer as rubbish as she did. This horrible behaviour reflects her heart.

Abysmal services as usual.Flight was delayed 2hrs, gate was moved 3 times, running around the airport with hundreds of people is an awful experience.Then on landing we ended up waiting another 40 mins on the aircraft, and at that point some passengers got so loud and angry it was like a circus.What a joke. Wizz Air is one of the worst and most disgusting airlines. Thank God I'm not the only one who hates them; there are many others. These employees are incompetent and racist, and now they're refusing to refund my ticket and the money I lost. I advise you to stay away from Wizz Air. I bought a ticket and paid for a seat, the seat didn't show up in the check-in process. I am not able to complete the check-in due to "overpayment".Wizz Air took my money, blocked my check-in, and refused to provide any support. Their bot sent me to Booking.com, Booking sent me back to them. I paid for a seat and was denied access to the flight. Scam-level business. Zero customer service. Avoid like the plague.

I am writing this to warn all passengers about a specific Wizz Air system glitch that they refuse to fix. I was unable to check in online for my flight from Marrakech to Budapest because the app displayed a "Flight Overpaid" error and locked me out.At the airport, the ground handling staff acknowledged this was a system error requiring HQ intervention, but still forced me to pay 495 MAD (approx. €47) for airport check-in just to get my boarding pass.When I submitted a claim with screenshots proving their system blocked me, Wizz Air Customer Service (Anton) sent a copy-paste refusal, claiming I should have called their expensive premium rate call center from abroad to fix their IT bug. Again they postponed and delayed the departure for more than 12 hours and again the scheduled time is delayed for another 2.5 hours. Avoid Wizz Air! They cancelled our flight back from Dalaman to Gatwick when we were due to board. Absolute chaos. No plan. I wish I had read the reviews.

On September 11, at Heraklion Airport in Crete, Greece, I accompanied a disabled friend on their flight from Heraklion to Bucharest, which was scheduled to depart at 9:20 p.m.    Unfortunately, the experience was marked by a lack of organization.    There is no designated desk for wheelchair assistance and we had to wait until check-in opened two hours before departure.   The main problem was the unavailability of a wheelchair.    When one was finally found the check in agent was extremely rude.    Despite the fact that the need for assistance was clearly stated on the ticket, she asked us in detail about the nature of my friend's disability.    After waiting another 30 minutes for the wheelchair, the agent refused to give priority to my friend, even though this is standard procedure for passengers with disabilities.    Instead, she insisted on serving others in line who did not have mobility issues.   The check-in agent threatened to delay us further if we continued to ask questions, which she eventually did.    In all my experience with airports, I have never encountered a situation where a disabled passenger is not given priority.   The blonde lady at check-in showed a clear lack of professionalism and understanding of boarding procedures.    In our opinion, she is not suited for the role and would greatly benefit from additional training.   I am deeply disappointed with Wizz Air and it seems that the service with this airline continues to deteriorate.

I had the worst customer service from WizzAir yesterday. I've been told to pay extra £68 for my luggage as my backpack was large. I have paid £40 for extra luggage [10 KG] and genuinely was in rush and didn't think I need to go to the check-in area at Luton airport and thought that I'll be allowed in with what I paid for. I didn't know where the check in was as never used that service at this airport.  The agent at boaridng gate was ultra rude and said you're either paying £68 or not boarding. When I wanted to go quickly to check in she said it's too late. I've got stressed and mumbled swear words to myself from stress as I have ADHD and forgot my medication yesterday. She said I abused her and denied my boarding. I went to customer services and another agent said I abuse their staff and it's unacceptable and that I have to pay for this. I was excuse me? As a result, I had to pay £300 extra for the flight with another airline today. I have never had more stress in my life related to travel. 0 understanding, the worst customer service I've ever experienced. I demand apology and full refund on my flight. Avoid this airline at all costs.
Always late but you have to arrive at the prison same time .. disgusting services.. food rubbish, seat belt never working.. i won't ever fly with this useless company again...
THIEVES, they DO NOT REFUND YOU even though they cancel flights. DO NOT BOOK

Ticket cancellation due to illness spend over €200 they are offering €29.99 refund. After emailed to clarify if this is correct the agent gone for weeks with no answer.I would rather pay more and get support and customer service available. Avoid at all cost!

Our flight was rerouted due to “weather conditions”, but Wizz Air’s handling of the situation was unacceptable.Instead of the scheduled 10:30 arrival in Budapest, by 8:00 PM we were still not there.Passengers were forced to wait two hours outdoors in winter conditions, then put on a bus that stood for hours at the Hungarian–Serbian border.There was no food, no drinks, no warm waiting area and no meaningful information provided.Weather may be an extraordinary circumstance, but failure to provide basic passenger care is not.
Thought I'll give Wizzair a try, but unfortunately this low rating seems to be true, use Wizzair at your own risk.

The website is confusing, drip pricing means that flights advertised as cheap are actually the same price as BA, and the app is completely unfit for purpose. I will be writing to trading standards re: this horrible airline.

Even 1 star is to much for this companyLoot of delays, we have to stay around 2 hours in the plane, also we wait more than 1 hour before boarding..They change the plane with different company without let us know with discomfort seats.. If you have other choice please avoid Wizz Airline!!!

If i could give 0 review, i would. Cancelled flight and then blamed the weather refusing to pay compensation. The real reason which was even explained to us on the airplane, was due to not having a staff member on board and exceeding staff shift working hours. Absolutely disgraceful lies, never ever again i fly with this company

I bought check in luggage instead of cabin carry on luggage. The price was the same or even higher. Unfortunately I did not check in and during boarding I was asked to pay again and triple amount of what I paid. 2 luggages. They were not oversized, fitting to standard and not overweighted. But they did not care. The claim was unsolved even writing to customer service. They fully refused to refund part of full amount.

I had booked a trip to Poland flying from Leeds. At the same time, I also booked a non-refundable hotel in Poland.A week later, I receive and email from Wizz informing me there has been a change to my flight. The change was instead of flying from Leeds - I must now fly from Luton. Luton is 184 miles away form my home!!I have been able to cancel the flights, although I'm still waiting on the refund being processed. I spoke with a very rude person on chat who said it could be 14 days for the refund.I have put a claim to Wizz for the £160 I am now out of pocket for the hotel, but they will not pay this. Basically said, their terms and conditions cover their backs for this type of claim. It seems absolutely ridiculous. I will escalate the complaint further but I can't see that I will be refunded the £160, that if I even get the cost of the flights back without them taking a fee. Never had an issue with an airline until now. DO NOT USE THIS COMPANY!

Terrible ancillary fees. Never in my life had I encountered a fee for checking in at the airport before. Online check-in is conveniently no longer available 3 hours to the flight, and sometimes the app produces errors during check-in which WizzAir takes no responsibility for.

Wizz air are scammers!!!I was charged €73 euros per 10kg case as we were told at the boarding gate we should have checked it in to go in the hold! Nowhere did we see that when you check-in on line you should then leave your 10kg (cabin bag) at check in when you arrive at the airport for it to go in the hold!! Very misleading and a way to scam many people when you get to the boarding gate! Also their staff don’t even know their company policies as whilst waiting for the boarding gate to open one member of staff checked our boarding passes and gave us priority stickers to go on our 10kg luggage and then when we got to the boarding gate desk his colleague refused us and ripped the stickers off and we were demanded to pay or refused our flight home! Absolutely disgusting behaviour and how Wizz air are legally allowed to carry on in this way I do not know! This company are ripping people off!!! Their website also doesn’t work, we continuously had issues trying to check in on line and get our boarding passes, it constantly said our password was wrong and an email to reset it never arrived!! Very poor service, we will never fly with Wizz air again and we were not the only ones being scammed in the queue to board the flight, there were many other disputing their rules due to luggage dimensions etc.  DO NOT FLY WITH WIZZ AIR

I would like to complain about the way the refund was granted for the cancellation of flights purchased directly from the company ; I should have had the right to choose the way to send the money, but this cannot be done from the form on the site. I paid with a bank card and I want the money sent to the same account and not in Wizz credits; they have not respected the conditions written on the site.It s impossible to contact compamy, unless you complete forms wich run to whatever tey want!

It's clear that people would have liked to give less than one star, but that's not possible. I had to cancel my flight, losing about 80% of what I paid.After almost a month, they still haven't refunded the small change owed, and they continue to send emails saying they've escalated the ticket for the refund.They also sent me an initial email saying the process had been completed, so be careful and check your bank statements. Should we assume they just got distracted?

✈️ Terrible experience with Wizz AirOur flight W9 3901 from Bacău to London Luton (5 Sept 2025, 00:20) was cancelled one hour after the scheduled time.We received an email saying the flight was rescheduled for 13:30 instead.After waiting at the airport, we were told there would be no hotel accommodation or transport provided, so we had to arrange and pay for everything ourselves. We also had the children with us. Weeks later, I received an email from the customer service saying that I needed to claim under another section - the one that I was recommended from a wizz phone representative to follow (no link provided, and I couldn`t find the proper section on the website) and that I will be refunded less than half of the expenses I encountered and offered no compensation. I also lost around £1,200 in income due to missed work sessions because of the flight rescheduling.Overall, the way this situation was handled was unacceptable.I do not recommend Wizz Air — very poor communication and no real support for passengers affected by cancellations.

This is a scam airlines!Never use this for your travel as they actually show a cheap ticket which people buy it but at the end of the travel with all the extras they are the expensive ones. They will ask for checkin online but their system would not allow you to do so and eventually you would pay 50 euro extras in the airport.A big NO to Wizz/ worst airlines.

On my flight from Vilnius to Larnaca, at the gates, just before boarding I was informed that I was "put on hold" and that they would let me in only after all the passengers have boarded the plane and a seat remained vacant for some reason. Although I had a boarding pass with a dedicated seat. No further explanation was provided. After gathering the information on my own I found out that if you choose not to buy a specific seat Wizzair has a right to refuse to let you board the plane. INSANE.

I purchased a Flex ticket with Wizz Air, paying extra specifically for the right to cancel and receive a refund. What followed was unacceptable.Despite cancelling online exactly as advised, my refund was issued only as in-app credit, not to my original payment method. I was later told that only phone cancellations qualify for refunds to the original card — a critical condition that is neither clear nor transparent at the point of purchase.Worse still, the credit expired after just three months, with no reminder, no warning, and no notification. The money simply vanished.This is not customer-centric. It is a designed trap: steer customers to online cancellation, issue restrictive credits, set a short expiry, and rely on silence to keep the funds.A Flex ticket should provide flexibility — not confusion, hidden conditions, and loss of money. This business model raises serious questions about fairness, transparency, and consumer protection.

Ryanair’s business model isn't about budget travel; it’s about greed and engineered confusion. The "low fare" is a complete illusion designed to lure you in before they hit you with a barrage of hidden fees and "surprises" at the gate.​The baggage information is intentionally opaque. They make it nearly impossible to understand when you actually need to drop a bag versus taking it to the gate, and they use that lack of clarity as a trap to extract more money. Their "strict rules" are just a lack of flexibility masquerading as policy, creating every possible opportunity to steal more money from passengers under the guise of "fees."​The experience on my return flight was the final straw. After being forced into a "self-check-in" process, we waited for over an hour because there was no one there to actually manage the bag belt or assist. If you’re going to automate a service, it needs to actually work. This level of inefficiency is staggering.​By the time you pay for all the "extras" they squeeze out of you, you end up paying significantly more than you would with a mid-tier airline. I’d much rather pay a fair, transparent price upfront to a company that actually provides the service you paid for without the constant fee-traps. What Ryanair does feels predatory and should honestly be illegal. This was the last time I fly with them.

I recently traveled on Ryan Air from Manchester to Belfast, return. The joy of having spent a few days with family, was dissipated at check in. With the multitude of emails warning me of my paid baggage allowance, I ensured that our bags were all the correct size and weight. We opted for an additional cabin bag and paid to carry on a bag with dimensions of 55 x 40 x 20 cm. Our journey to Belfast was seamless.On our return flight, the gate agent asked us to place our bag in the measuring device. When we did he said it was too big and we would have to pay to check the bag. I removed a jumper which I was going to wear on the flight anyway, and remeasured. The bag fit. However he said unless we paid we could not board. I wish we had recorded the entire interaction. His rudeness escalated throughout our interaction. We were left with no option but to be held ransom and pay the £75 based on his whim. We asked his name and he refused to provide it. I can only assume this is because he has had so many complaints from other customers and he is concerned about losing his job. I think that anyone with his level of aggression and rudeness should NEVER be in a customer facing position. To add to the insult of the entire interaction with this gate agent, when we arrived at the bottom of the steps and asked the airport crew to take our, now checked, bag; they responded by telling us to just carry it on board. Clearly our rude gate agent was the only person who felt our bag was oversized. When we boarded, we were chatting with the cabin crew who knew why we were delayed boarding and said they witnessed his rudeness. See attached photos of our bag’s dimensions and a photo of it in the measuring device. I would not only like a refund of £75, I would like an apology for the way we were treated and spoken to. I would love to know that the rude young man has been dismissed, but that’s beyond hope, as he refused to reveal his identity and rather be an anonymous bully.

If you're looking for the worst flying experience imaginable, Ryanair delivers — in all the wrong ways. Eye rolls, dismissive tones, and a complete lack of courtesy were the norm.- Zero Support: Need help? Good luck. Whether it’s at the airport or online, getting in touch with Ryanair is like trying to contact a ghost. No one picks up, no one replies, and no one cares.- Disrespectful Attitude: I’ve never felt so belittled by airline staff. Questions were met with sarcasm or outright indifference. It’s as if helping passengers was beneath them.- Chaotic Boarding: The boarding process was a mess — no clear instructions, long delays, and a stampede-like atmosphere that felt more like a cattle drive than a flight.- Overall Experience: Stressful, degrading, and infuriating. I recently had the opportunity to compare Ryanair with a scheduled airline on a trip from Madeira to London via Oporto. The low-cost airline was all about squeezing the passenger out of money...from their website's push for additional charges to comfort on board. Ryanair's miniscule baggage allowance was very poor in comparison to the scheduled carrier. If you pay for a bigger carry-on bag you get a similar allowance as the other carrier's standard allowance). My brother and I were denied boarding at Alicante Airport in circumstances that were completely unnecessary and unjustified.We were already speaking with a Ryanair staff member to pay for a cabin bag that had not been accepted. We were fully willing and able to pay immediately, and there was absolutely no reason to prevent us from boarding. The issue appeared to be on its way to being resolved.Another female gate agent then intervened. After speaking to the colleague in Spanish, which we understood, it became clear that she was instructing him not to accept the payment and that we would not be allowed to board. Her intervention was neither transparent, professional, nor fair. She seemed to impose her decision unilaterally, despite there being no valid reason to block our boarding. It gave the clear impression that she was exercising authority beyond what the situation required.We were understandably upset, because we were actively trying to resolve the issue. Our reaction was later described as “aggressive,” which we strongly dispute. In our view, the situation escalated because of the way it was handled by this staff member, not because we refused to cooperate.The other staff members did not seem comfortable with the way it was being handled, but no one stepped in, leaving us entirely at the mercy of a single person’s decision.As a result, we were unexpectedly forced to spend the night in the airport, despite being ready to pay and board. Children aged 10 to 13 were waiting for our arrival, which made the consequences of this arbitrary decision even more distressing.What makes it worse is that Ryanair later chose to simply repeat the staff version of events instead of properly addressing the fact that we were ready to pay and had no reason to be denied boarding.This was not a normal baggage issue. It was a situation that could easily have been resolved, but was instead escalated unnecessarily by a staff member acting beyond her authority.

On 15 February 2026, I travelled on Ryanair flight FR486 from Marseille (scheduled 12:55, Port A).At boarding, a staff member required me to place my cabin bag in the baggage sizer. I immediately complied. I was travelling with a single cabin bag, and it fit fully within the measuring device.However, several passengers ahead of me were allowed to board with two bags without any size check or intervention. I did not observe their baggage being measured. The selective enforcement of the policy was visible and created an uncomfortable situation, as I appeared to be singled out despite complying fully with the rules.Although I was not charged any additional fee, the inconsistent application of the baggage policy caused unnecessary stress, embarrassment, and inconvenience during boarding.I submitted a formal complaint to Ryanair requesting:Financial compensation for the inconvenience, a written explanation of the criteria used by staff, confirmation of the measures Ryanair will take to ensure uniform and non-discriminatory enforcement of its baggage rules.The response I received did not address these points. Instead, Ryanair simply restated its general baggage policy, explaining that passengers must comply with size limits:"Dear Customer,As previously advised, you must adhere to the baggage allowance specified on your booking. If your cabin bag exceeds the maximum dimensions, it will be tagged and placed in the aircraft hold, subject to a gate bag fee. You may collect your bag upon arrival at the baggage carousel."I therefore reiterate my request for:- Financial compensation for the inconvenience and distress caused;- A written explanation of the criteria used for baggage checks at the gate;- Confirmation of the steps Ryanair will implement to ensure consistent and equal application of its baggage policy.M. Antunes

On 1 March 2026, Ryanair destroyed our UPPAbaby Vista stroller (value €1,190) while it was in their care. I was travelling alone with a 5-month-old baby and a 2.5-year-old toddler. The stroller was handed over in perfect condition at the gate and returned completely broken — a structural part had snapped off, making it impossible to attach the bassinet. We had no choice but to buy a replacement abroad, with a baby who cannot be carried indefinitely.Ryanair's response: first they claimed the stroller was "inherently unsuitable for carriage" — an item they routinely accept at the gate. Then they demanded manufacturer documentation that has no basis in the Montreal Convention. Their final offer: €50 for a €1,190 stroller destroyed by their baggage handlers. Ref: 73472267 / PIR: AGPFR25994.On the same trip (1 March 2026), Ryanair also lost our checked luggage. We travelled with a 5-month-old and a 2.5-year-old — all their clothing and essentials were in the missing suitcase. Ryanair's own staff told us to buy emergency items in the meantime. The bag arrived 2.5 days later, on the night of Tuesday 3 March.Ryanair's response: they claimed the bag was returned "within 24 hours" — which is factually false — and refused all reimbursement on that basis. When I pointed out this was incorrect and cited Article 19 of the Montreal Convention, they simply repeated their rejection without addressing a single argument. Ref: 73773645 / PIR: AGPFR18549.

One star because zero is not possibleVery negative check-in experience at Rome Fiumicino (Ryanair)Our last check-in experience at Rome Fiumicino Airport was extremely frustrating and unacceptable.Upon arrival at the airport, we needed to check in two suitcases and one snowboard for transport in the aircraft hold. We were first forced to queue for over 30 minutes to “skip the lines” and use the self-service kiosks to weigh and label our luggage. Several kiosks were out of order (red crosses on the screens), yet only one staffed counter was open. At the baggage drop-off area, two Ryanair attendants were standing aside chatting, showing no initiative to assist passengers.When it was finally our turn, an error occurred during the process (we initially assumed it was due to the snowboard’s dimensions). We were told to “seek assistance,” but no staff member was willing to help us. Instead, they repeatedly stated that we had to do it ourselves, that it was “not difficult,” and that everything was “self-explanatory.”Many passengers around us were experiencing similar issues and growing increasingly anxious about missing their flights. Only after persistent requests did one attendant reluctantly start helping. At the same time, the other attendant informed the long line behind us that they should go queue at the counters instead — despite those lines being extremely long.When assistance finally reached us, we were then told to join the back of that same long queue we had already been redirected away from. We nearly missed our flight. The waiting time felt endless and the situation was highly stressful.The overall attitude of the staff — marked by rudeness, inefficiency, lack of empathy, and even a sense of amusement at passengers’ distress — was shocking and deeply disappointing.

I recently flew the Dublin (DUB) to Manchester (MAN) route (Jan 19, 2026) and the experience was a textbook example of poor communication and operational failure.1. The "Pier D" Wild Goose ChaseDespite a quick security clearance, the gate management at Dublin Terminal 1 was a disaster. Passengers were redirected across Pier D multiple times (Gates 109 → 107 → 118 → 102). A last-minute aircraft swap (9H-VVD) caused a total disconnect between the automated boards and physical reality. We were marched to the end of the pier for a different aircraft, only to be walked back to gate 102 where our plane had been sitting the entire time at stand 107R.2. Arbitrary Seating & In-Flight FailuresBoarding was chaotic due to last-minute seat reassignments on the app (shifting people from Row 2 to Row 16 without clear cause). Worse still, once in the air, the crew couldn't fulfill any duty-free orders. Apparently, following the aircraft swap, the incoming crew locked the carts but didn't hand over the keys. It’s a spectacular "own goal" for an airline to lose all ancillary revenue simply because they couldn't open a trolley.3. Reliability IssuesRyanair has shifted from a "punctual flying bus" to a service prone to lengthy, unexplained delays. Their ability to communicate in real-time is woeful.Verdict: If you are seeking reliability—especially over Easter 2026—save yourself the "Gate 100-series" stress. Take the Holyhead-Dublin Ferry instead. It’s a superior alternative to the unpredictability of DUB T1 and Ryanair's current service levels.

I am extremely frustrated by Ryanair's unethical and deceptive dynamic pricing system. This consisted of a £45 change fee plus an inflated new ticket price of £181.99.However, when I opened a completely separate, standard search for the exact same flight on 17/09/2026 as a new customer, the public price was only £61.55.This means Ryanair deliberately inflates the base fare of the flight by nearly 200% exclusively for existing customers who try to modify their booking. This is a blatant, anti-consumer trap designed to milk extra money from passengers. Beware of this modification scam!

In the past three weeks, I’ve taken around eight flights across three continents. I experienced delays of over three hours and other issues along the way, but nothing comes close to how unacceptable this Ryanair flight was.From the very beginning, it was clear there was serious mismanagement. Boarding took place during heavy rain, and passengers were left standing outside for about 10 minutes, completely exposed, with no proper organization or care. By the time I got on the plane, I was soaked, freezing, and physically shaking.It didn’t get better onboard. The aircraft was clearly not cleaned, and I had to wipe down my own seat before sitting. Still trying to warm up, I attempted to buy a cup of tea only to be told the machine wasn’t working. Even basic paid services failed.What happened after landing was far worse.My Swiss carry-on was returned covered in mud, which is already unacceptable handling. But the real shock came when I opened it at the hotel: every single item inside was wet and muddy. This is not normal mishandling, this means my luggage was left sitting in water and mud long enough for it to seep through the zippers and destroy everything inside.This was a business trip, and I had packed brand new clothes. All of them were ruined. I was left with nothing clean or dry to wear—not even my arrival outfit, since that was already soaked during boarding.This is not about low-cost travel anymore. This is negligence. Saving a small amount on a ticket is meaningless when your belongings are treated this carelessly and end up destroyed.This experience was unacceptable, unprofessional, and completely avoidable. I will not fly with Ryanair again, and I strongly warn others: think carefully before trusting this airline with your belongings.That said, I want to be fair, the captain and onboard crew were polite and professional despite the situation. As digital nomads, my wife and I travel constantly around Europe and have flown with dozens of airlines across multiple countries and airports. Ryanair is, without a doubt, the most exploitative and unpleasant of them all. We paid for the “Priority” fare specifically to bring our hand luggage on board (the small bag under the seat + the wheeled cabin suitcase of standard size in the overhead bin), but at the gate, they forced us to check it because it supposedly “exceeded the size.”It’s ridiculous! Ryanair deliberately reduces the allowed size for the larger bag to 55x40x20 cm (including wheels and handles), which is smaller than the international IATA standard (56x45x25 cm) used by most airlines. Many normal cabin suitcases measure 55x40x23 or similar, and although they fit perfectly in the overhead bins of their Boeing 737 planes, they use strict sizers at the gate to “catch” you. They charged us around 35-70€ per bag (depending on the airport) to check it at the last minute, after we’d already paid for Priority!It’s a shameless way to extract extra money. They know people arrive at the airport with no choice and pay to avoid missing the flight. We’ve seen entire families crying and arguing at the gate over the same issue. As frequent travelers, this feels abusive and hostile. Other low-cost carriers like EasyJet or Wizz Air are more flexible and honest with their measurements.Ryanair offers cheap flights, yes, but in the end, you pay more in hidden fees. If you’re traveling ultra-light with just a small backpack, it might be worth it, but for nomads like us who need a bit more, it’s constant stress. I recommend avoiding them and paying a little extra for decent airlines. We won’t fly with them again!

Our Ryanair flight from Alicante to Berlin on 06.02.2026 was significantly delayed and diverted to Hannover airport due to weather. I understand these situations happen, but what followed was unacceptable.The Ryanair staff announced that we will be redirected to Hannover airport only AFTER all passengers already boarded the plane, which understandably caused surprise and stir among the passengers. At that point, we were basically told we can either stay and continue, or leave the plane if unhappy. The people leaving the plane and the resulting organisational mess led to a delay of approximately 2.5 hours before we could take off, during which we were not offered any complimentary water or refreshments. Instead, staff told us that any food or drinks would have to be paid for.Upon landing in Hannover, there was a huge queue outside the airport in winter cold waiting for replacements buses to take us to Berlin. Many of us (including children and elderly) ended up waiting longer than an hour before we could board one of the buses. Again, no assistance, no shelter from the cold, no proper communication.We finally arrived at Berlin airport after 2am, over 7 hours later than scheduled. Under EU Regulation 261/2004, airlines must provide care to passengers during long delays, regardless of the cause. During the entire journey, I did not experience this care as adequately provided.I would not recommend Ryanair to anyone who values being treated with basic dignity when things go wrong.

However, a group of around 20 passengers from the Orthodox Jewish community, who were seated nearby and boarding later, were all stopped and required to have their bags checked. Several were then charged for their luggage being marginally over the size allowance—by what appeared to be just one or two centimetres.The contrast in treatment was stark and deeply concerning. It appeared that this group was singled out in a way that others were not. I have never witnessed something that felt so clearly discriminatory.As I walked to board, I passed a young couple from this group repacking their belongings after being stopped. I commented on how unfair the situation seemed, and the woman responded by apologising. That moment stayed with me—it was upsetting to see someone feel the need to apologise in response to what appeared to be unfair treatment.This experience left me genuinely saddened. In today’s world, there should be no place for behaviour that appears discriminatory or biased. I strongly urge Ryanair to review how boarding procedures are carried out and to ensure that all staff are consistently applying policies in a fair, respectful, and non-discriminatory manner.No passenger should feel they are being treated differently because of who they are.

My recent experience with Ryanair was incredibly disappointing and stressful.On 15 June, I was denied boarding on my flight from London Luton to Dublin due to overbooking. I was placed on standby without any warning and only informed after boarding had finished that there was no seat available for me. The situation was upsetting and very poorly handled.What made things worse was the lack of support at the airport. Ryanair staff told me to travel to Gatwick for a later flight and specifically instructed me to take a taxi, assuring me that the cost would be reimbursed. No alternative transport options were mentioned, no explanations were given, and no help was offered. As someone unfamiliar with Luton Airport and already under pressure, I relied entirely on the information I was given.Months later, after submitting all my receipts, including a very expensive taxi fare, I was told that this cost would not be reimbursed because public transport was supposedly available. This was never communicated to me on the day. Being told one thing at the airport and then having Ryanair deny responsibility afterwards feels unfair and misleading.On top of this, the claims process took over six months to receive any meaningful response, despite several follow-ups from my side. Communication was poor, responses were generic, and I felt completely ignored.While compensation under EU regulations was eventually acknowledged, the overall experience, from denied boarding to misinformation from staff, refusal to honour what I was told, and excessive delays, was far below an acceptable standard of customer service.This experience has seriously damaged my trust in Ryanair.

Flight from London Stansted to Bergamo – 28/08/26, boarding around 08:35During boarding at Stansted, I experienced extremely poor and unnecessarily confrontational treatment from gate staff.My backpack fit into the size frame as requested, yet I was still told it was “too bulky.” I removed my ski helmet and attached it to the backpack (something I have done before without issue) and was then accused of having “too many items.” From that point on, the situation escalated publicly in front of other passengers during active boarding.Staff showed a complete lack of flexibility, common sense, and agile problem-solving. Instructions were inconsistent and poorly explained, with multiple staff intervening instead of calmly resolving a simple issue. Other passengers even attempted to help, highlighting how poorly the situation was being handled.To avoid further disruption, I briefly wore the helmet, only to be told—without any clear reference to written policy—that this was not allowed either. I eventually walked through the gate wearing a helmet, which was humiliating. Once onboard, I placed the helmet back into my backpack without any problem, proving the entire situation was avoidable.I am a 55-year-old customer and a nervous flyer. I expected basic respect, flexibility, and clear communication—not public embarrassment caused by rigid, box-ticking behaviour and poor staff training. This was an unnecessary stress and a very bad start to my holiday.Ryanair urgently needs better gate staff training, particularly around customer service, consistency, and agile, practical decision-making.

Extremely Rude and Unprofessional Service at Check-in – Ibiza to LondonI recently had one of the worst travel experiences with Ryanair when flying from Ibiza to London. As a Mauritian passport holder, I do not require a visa to travel to the UK. At the check-in desk, however, the staff insisted that I needed one.Despite me explaining this fact and even showing my approved UK Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA) — the check-in agent and the so-called “manager” continued to insist that I needed a non-existent “form with my photo on it.” When I asked how to obtain such a form, the manager dismissively told me to “go find it yourself” and accused me of blocking the line. He was incredibly rude, unhelpful, and unprofessional throughout.When I tried to escalate, he arrogantly stated he was the manager and there was no one else to speak to. It was only after visiting the Ryanair office that another Ryanair staff member confirmed that I was indeed correct: I did not need a visa, and I was allowed to check in. Even then, the manager avoided eye contact and showed zero accountability for his behavior.This whole ordeal was unnecessarily stressful and humiliating. A manager should know the basic entry requirements for passengers and, more importantly, should know how to communicate respectfully. If your own managers are misinformed and rude, what can passengers expect from the rest of the staff?Ryanair needs to urgently improve staff training, especially for managers, on both customer service and immigration requirements. This experience has left me extremely disappointed and unwilling to fly with Ryanair again.

Humiliating treatment by ground staff – ignored by RyanairOn 24 August 2025, at Toulouse-Blagnac Airport (Gate 7, FR 1799 Toulouse–Lisbon), I was subjected to humiliating and coercive treatment by ground staff (Aviapartner, contracted by Ryanair).	•	The gate was announced late, after 17:50, despite being scheduled to close at 18:10.	•	At 18:32, while still in the boarding queue, I was pressured to pay an additional bag fee under threats that my ticket would be cancelled in 30 seconds.	•	Staff confiscated my passport and phone, refusing to return them until I gave my card details. They rejected Apple Pay, pressured me to call my boyfriend (already forced to board without me) to get his card details, and deliberately created panic so I couldn’t even adjust my bag.	•	Other passengers with larger bags were allowed to board without issue, making the process arbitrary and unfair.	•	Even Ryanair cabin crew (including supervisor Gustavo) apologized to me and called the behavior “outrageous.” A fellow passenger also offered testimony.I filed a detailed complaint with Ryanair, asking for an investigation, refund of the unfair charge, and compensation. The reply I received was a generic copy-paste statement about “Ryanair being multicultural” that ignored every single point of my complaint, including the threats, document retention, and witness statements.This incident ruined the end of my holiday. I am a frequent flyer, but after this degrading treatment and Ryanair’s refusal to take responsibility, I will avoid this airline in the future and warn others to do the same.

Ryanair are the least customer centred airline I have ever flown with and I have flow in and out of 127 different countries over 50 years and their service is usually pretty bad. Most of the staff were politeish  and we had a laugh about it as being "the most checked bag in history." Until I boarded at Stansted where the staff were aggressive and rude. So I complained. The response totally ignored the core of my complaint which was why did the checks have to be so rigorous and why were the staff so rude. Ryanair maintain that they can do whatever they like about checking my bag because it says so in their terms and conditions. They also claim (laughably) that their staff are highly trained in customer service standards. The implication being that they are never rude or aggressive. We, their customers know different!

The assistant I met not only made me measure my bag but also made fun of me for five minutes. less than one star is possible. as an American, I am used to different airlines ranging from low-cost to high-cost and understand that you pay for what you get. However, I think that these cheap UK based airlines get away with just plain bad business practices. On top of not allowing cancellations, if you decide to then change your flight to a different date that costs the same as your original booking, you would think they would change it for you for no fee. But no, they charge you a fee way over the cost of the actual flight and a customer support AI bot says to just buy a new flight... when you've already paid for a flight. Just horrible services.

Avoid Ryanair at all costs! This isn’t an airline; it’s a predatory business model designed to bleed people dry. They charged me an outrageous 55€ fee just to print a boarding pass because I was slightly late for the online check-in.​But the extortion doesn't stop there. They hover around like vultures, waiting to charge you extra if your bag is even a millimeter over their ridiculous dimensions. Once you finally get on the plane, the misery continues. The seats are clearly built for dwarves, with zero legroom and no comfort whatsoever. It’s an insulting experience from start to finish.​To top it all off, they had the nerve to stress us out by claiming the flight was overbooked, even after we paid their 'fines.' They literally earn money from human suffering and anxiety. It is a company with no soul and no respect for its passengers. Please, do yourself a favor: boycott this airline and fly with anyone else. Paying a bit more for a normal airline is worth it just to avoid the humiliation and the hidden costs of these bloodsuckers.

I am writing to express my deep dissatisfaction with my recent experience with Ryanair. The airline advertises low-cost tickets to attract customers, but in reality, it exploits passengers through unfair and excessive charges during the check-in process.My original ticket cost only around £70, and I made sure to check in online two days in advance. However, the Ryanair mobile app failed repeatedly, preventing me from completing the check-in despite multiple attempts. When I arrived at the airport early to resolve the issue, the airline staff insisted that I pay over £50 just to check in at the counter — almost doubling the ticket price.This situation was completely not my fault. I had every intention to check in online, but Ryanair’s faulty app made it impossible. It is unacceptable that passengers are penalised for the company’s technical failure.To make matters worse, the airport ticket check agent, named: James was extremely rude and unhelpful. Instead of assisting us or trying to understand the situation, he only pressured us to pay the additional fee. His attitude was unprofessional and disrespectful, leaving us no choice but to pay the extra charge.This experience has left me feeling frustrated and deceived. Ryanair’s conduct in this case amounts to misleading advertising and unfair business practice, using low ticket prices to lure customers, only to charge excessive fees later under unreasonable circumstances.I urge Ryanair to:1. Refund the unfair £50+ check-in fee, and2. Improve its app reliability and customer service training for airport staff.Until then, I will not recommend Ryanair to others and will be filing a formal complaint with the UK Civil Aviation Authority (CAA) and relevant consumer protection bodies.

The airport's and Ryanair’s attitude toward passengers is inhumane! Yesterday, I was waiting at the airport from 4 a.m. for a Ryanair flight scheduled for 6 a.m. to Catania. We spent two hours on the plane, then were told to leave and wait another two hours for further information. Our flight kept being postponed, and there were no voice announcements about Catania. At 1 p.m., we unofficially learned that boarding would begin soon, but due to a lack of information, one passenger didn't make it, and from 1:30 to 2:30 p.m., while we were already on the plane, they searched for his suitcase. At 2:30 p.m., we were told to wait another two hours for de-icing. In the meantime, we were promised that we would definitely fly out that day. At 4:30 PM, we learned that the pilot's shift had been overscheduled and that another plane was arriving to take us. I checked Flightradar and it was going the opposite direction! The pilot made the announcement after the passengers were the first to learn where the promised plane was going, saying that the airport had suddenly closed again and that we had to wait to be let off the plane. We waited two hours for the stairs to be provided, and in the meantime, the police arrived and escorted us as we disembarked. We were locked inside the plane – children and the elderly included – for over five hours, with no way out, no water, or food (which could be purchased for 5 euros!).Łamanie praw pasażerów, okłamywanie ludzi, spowodowanie że ludzie czekali 12 godizn na lotnisku w tym 5 godizn w zamknięciu w samolocie nie może przejść przejść bez konsekwencji dla Ryanaira i lotniska!The Ryanair crew and the airport lied to us from 6 a.m. onward. We were left stranded – we couldn't get any information about a flight reschedule or transportation or overnight stays at the hotel, which was our right. We wanted to collect our luggage and were informed after an hour's wait that our bags would be available at 5 a.m. the following day and that we had to sleep at the airport. The police present were indifferent to the fact that the children would be forced to sleep on a cold floor. No one from the staff was willing to provide any practical information. We were locked in the baggage claim area, unable to get food or go to the customer service desk to file a complaint and demand that our rights be respected. Only under pressure from desperate passengers did the airport allow us to leave the baggage claim area and go to buy food. We finally picked up our bags at 9 p.m., which proves that we were lied to the whole time. Passenger rights were violated, such as the right to receive information, the right to care (refreshments, meals, accommodation as appropriate) and the right to chose between reimbursement of fares or be re-routed to final destination. You broke our rights as passengers, you lied to us - we will fight for compensation, because spending 14 hours at the airport and 5 hours locked in a plane cannot pass without any consequences for both the airport and Ryanair!!

I  am utterly disappointed and frustrated with Ryanair customer service. Today, around 5pm, I called to add special assistance to my parents’ booking because my father is a wheelchair user. My parents are elderly and do not speak English, so I wanted to ensure that when I drop them at the airport, someone will be available to guide them through the airport and to the gate/aircraft.I also wanted to confirm whether my father, who has a pacemaker, would need any specific documentation. Unfortunately, I never even got the chance to ask this because the conversation quickly became stressful.The agent I spoke to, named Olivia,  was extremely rude and had a very entitled, superior attitude. I asked several times if she could please speak more slowly because she was talking very fast and I was struggling to understand. Instead of helping, she kept repeating the same information and did not listen to what I was trying to explain.When I made the booking online, the assistance request section only allows you to select “one level of assistance”, for example:* Passenger requires assistance through the airport to the boarding gate (WCHR)* Passenger requires assistance through the airport and lift on/off the aircraft seat (WCHC)Because I could only select one option, I chose the second one (WCHC) for my father and called customer service to add the first level of assistance as well.However, the agent misunderstood the situation from the start. She kept saying that I wanted to add assistance for my mother, which I never said. I repeatedly explained that the assistance request was for my father, but she continued insisting on things I had not asked for. When I tried to clarify the situation, she became impatient and eventually said the assistance I asked was there and that they would disconnect the call.I ended the call feeling completely unheard, uncomfortable, and ignored.I tried calling customer service again to confirm the assistance with another agent, but now my calls are automatically redirected to the website. It feels as though my number has been blocked, which is extremely concerning.At no point was I rude or impolite. I was simply trying to ensure proper assistance for two elderly people, one of whom uses a wheelchair and has a pacemaker.It is shocking to see such a lack of empathy, patience, and professionalism when dealing with situations involving vulnerable passengers.If I had the option to fly my parents with another airline, I absolutely would. Unfortunately, Ryanair is the only airline flying from the closest airport available to them.This experience has been deeply disappointing.

I booked a simple ticket for my uncle to travel from Germany to the UK. The booking clearly showed a 10kg bag included, which any normal passenger would assume is a small carry-on bag.However, when he arrived at the airport, Ryanair claimed the 10kg bag was actually a “checked bag” and not allowed in the cabin. Because of this confusing and unclear wording, he was charged an additional €60 at the gate for the exact same bag he had already paid for.This is a designed confusion trap. It is not accidental. Ryanair uses two almost identical labels:“10kg Checked Bag”“10kg Cabin Bag”Most people will assume “10kg bag included” means carry-on. Ryanair knows this, and they profit from the misunderstanding. A £20 flight became £60 because of a misleading baggage policy that is not explained properly during booking.We have already filed a formal complaint and will escalate to AviationADR / European Consumer Centre if this is not resolved. Passengers should not be forced to pay duplicate baggage fees due to unclear wording.This experience feels dishonest, predatory, and designed to take advantage of travellers, especially those who are not fluent in English.I recommend anyone booking with Ryanair to double-check every baggage line or be prepared to pay unexpected fees at the airport
